Matthew24:51 Then the master will punish him a lot. He will put the bad servant with those people who do not obey him. The people in that place will cry and they will bite their teeth together.’ 
Matthew25:1 Jesus said, ‘I will tell you another story to show what the kingdom of heaven is like. There were ten young women who were going to a marriage party. They took their lamps with them and they went to meet the man who was marrying a wife." 
Matthew25:2 Five of these young women were silly. The other five young women were wise. 
Matthew25:3 The silly women took their lamps with them, but they did not take any extra oil with them." 
Matthew25:4 The five wise women took jars of oil with their lamps. 
Matthew25:5 The man did not come to the marriage party for a long time. So all the young women became tired, and they went to sleep." 
Matthew25:6 In the middle of the night, someone shouted out, “The man is coming! Come out to meet him!”" 
Matthew25:7 The ten young women woke up. Then they prepared their lamps to make them give light. 
Matthew25:8 The five silly young women said to the other five young women, “Give us some of your oil, because our lamps are not giving much light.”" 
Matthew25:9 The wise women replied, “If we did that, then there would not be enough oil for all of us. You must go instead to the people who sell oil. You must buy some oil for yourselves.”" 
Matthew25:10 So these five women went to buy some oil. While they were away, the man arrived for his marriage party. The five wise women were ready. They went with him into the house for the party. Then the servants shut the door." 
Matthew25:11 Later, the five silly women also came to the house. They said, “Sir, Sir, please open the door for us.”" 
Matthew25:12 But he replied, “I tell you, I do not know you.” ’" 
Matthew25:13 Then Jesus said to the people, ‘So you must watch carefully. You do not know on what day or at what time I will return.’" 
Matthew25:14 Jesus said, ‘The kingdom of heaven is also like a man who began a journey. Before the master went away, he told his servants to come to him. He said to them, “Take care of all my money and all my things for me.”" 
Matthew25:15 The master gave one servant 5,000 gold coins. He gave another servant 2,000 gold coins, and he gave 1,000 gold coins to another servant. The master gave to each servant what he would be able to use well. Then he went away on his journey." 
Matthew25:16 The servant who had received 5,000 gold coins went out immediately. He bought and sold things with the money. When he did this, he got 5,000 more gold coins." 
Matthew25:17 The servant who had received 2,000 gold coins did the same thing. This servant got 2,000 more gold coins." 
Matthew25:18 But the servant who had received only 1,000 gold coins did something different. He went outside and he dug a hole in the ground. He buried the money that his master had given him." 
Matthew25:19 After a long time, the master came home. He asked the servants to come to him. He wanted to know how much money they had now." 
Matthew25:20 The servant who had received 5,000 gold coins came to his master. The servant said, “Master, you gave me 5,000 gold coins. Now I have an extra 5,000 gold coins.”" 
Matthew25:21 The master said to this servant, “You are a good servant and you have done really well. You were careful with a small amount of money. Now I will give you authority over many more things. Come now, and we will be happy together.”" 
Matthew25:22 The servant who had received 2,000 gold coins also came to his master. The servant said, “Master, you gave me 2,000 gold coins. Now I have an extra 2,000 gold coins.”" 
Matthew25:23 The master said to this servant, “You are a good servant and you have done really well. You were careful with a small amount of money. Now I will give you authority over many more things. Come now, and we will be happy together”" 
Matthew25:24 The servant who had received 1,000 gold coins then came to his master. The servant said, “Master, I know what you are like. You tell people what they should do all the time. You take food from fields where you did not put any seed in the ground. You get fruit from trees that you did not plant." 
Matthew25:25 So I was afraid of you. I went and I buried your gold coins in the ground. Look! Here they are. You can have your money back again.” 
Matthew25:26 The master said to him, “You are a bad and lazy servant. You know what I am like, do you? I get food from fields where I did not put any seeds. I get fruit from trees that I did not plant. You knew all that." 
Matthew25:27 So you should have put the money into a bank. Then, when I came home, I could have received my money back from the bank, with some extra money.”" 
Matthew25:28 Then the master said to his other servants, “Take the 1,000 gold coins from this bad servant. Give them to the man who now has 10,000 coins." 
Matthew25:29 Some people have received good things. They will all receive even more and have lots of good things. Some other people have nothing. Those people will lose even the little bit that they do have. 
Matthew25:30 This servant cannot work for me any longer. Take him outside and throw him into a dark place. There, people will cry and they will bite their teeth together.” ’" 
Matthew25:31 Jesus said, ‘I, the Son of Man, will return one day. Then people will see that I am great and powerful. All God's angels will come with me. I will sit on my great throne as king." 
Matthew25:32 People from all the countries in the world will come together in front of me. I will put them into two groups. I will do it in the same way that a shepherd puts his sheep and goats into two groups. 
Matthew25:33 I will put the sheep on my right side and the goats on my left side. 
Matthew25:34 The king will say to those people who are on his right side, “Come here. My Father has been good to you. He has prepared a place for you in his kingdom. That kingdom has been ready for you since the time that God made the world." 
Matthew25:35 When I was hungry, you gave me some food to eat. When I was thirsty, you gave me some water to drink. When I was visiting your town, you asked me to stay in your home." 
Matthew25:36 When I did not have enough clothes, you gave me some clothes to wear. When I was ill, you took care of me. When I was in prison, you came to visit me there.”" 
Matthew25:37 Then the people on the right side will say to the king, “Master, when did we see that you were hungry and we gave you some food? When did we see that you were thirsty and we gave you some water to drink?" 
Matthew25:38 When did we ask you to stay in our home because you were alone? When did we give you some clothes to wear? 
Matthew25:39 When did we take care of you because you were ill? When did we come to visit you in prison?” 
Matthew25:40 The king will answer them, “I tell you this. You did all these things to help other people. They were not important people, but they were my friends. When you helped them, then you also helped me.”" 
Matthew25:41 Then the king will say to those people who are at his left side, “Go away from me. God has decided to punish you. He has prepared a fire that will burn for ever. He has prepared it for Satan and his servants. You will also go into that fire." 
Matthew25:42 I was hungry but you did not give me any food to eat. I was thirsty but you did not give me anything to drink. 
Matthew25:43 I visited your town but you did not ask me to stay in your home. I did not have enough clothes but you did not give me anything to wear. I was ill but you did not take care of me. I was in prison but you did not come to visit me.” 
Matthew25:44 Then these other people will say, “Master, when did we see you and you were hungry, or you were thirsty? When did we see you alone or without clothes? When did we see you and you were ill, or in prison? When did you need our help and we did not help you?”" 
Matthew25:45 Then the king will reply, “I tell you this. You did not help the people who are my friends. They were not important people. But when you did not help them, you also did not help me.”" 
Matthew25:46 Then those people on the king's left side will go away to the place where God will punish them for ever. But the good people on the king's right side will live for ever with God.’ 
Matthew26:1 When Jesus finished saying all these things, he said to his disciples," 
Matthew26:2 ‘You know that after two days it will be the time of the Passover festival. Then those people who are against me will put me into the power of the rulers. They will fix me, the Son of Man, to a cross to kill me.’" 
Matthew26:3 Then the leaders of the priests and the important Jews met together in the house of the most important priest. His name was Caiaphas. 
Matthew26:4 They wanted to kill Jesus. But they wanted to take hold of him secretly. So they tried to decide how they could do that. 
Matthew26:5 They said to each other, ‘We do not want to take hold of him during the Passover festival. If we do that, the people will be angry and they may fight against us.’" 
Matthew26:6 Then Jesus went to Bethany and he visited Simon at his house. At one time, Simon had had a bad skin disease." 
Matthew26:7 While Jesus was eating a meal there, a woman came into the house. She brought a small stone jar with her. The jar contained expensive oil with a very nice smell. She poured the oil over Jesus' head while he sat there." 
Matthew26:8 Jesus' disciples saw what the woman had done. They became angry and they said, ‘This woman should not have wasted the oil." 
Matthew26:9 She could have sold it for a lot of money. Then she could have given the money to poor people.’ 
Matthew26:10 Jesus knew what his disciples were saying. So he said to them, ‘Do not cause trouble for her. She has done a good thing to me." 
Matthew26:11 You will always have poor people with you. But you will not always have me with you. 
Matthew26:12 She has poured oil over my body. So now I will be ready for people to bury me. 
Matthew26:13 I tell you this. Everywhere in the world people will speak about this good news. At the same time, they will also tell people about the good thing that this woman has done. And so people will remember her.’" 
Matthew26:14 Judas Iscariot was one of Jesus' 12 apostles. He went to see the leaders of the priests 
Matthew26:15 and he asked them, ‘How much money will you give me if I help you to take hold of Jesus?’ The priests gave Judas 30 silver coins." 
Matthew26:16 Judas then waited for the right moment to help them to take hold of Jesus. 
Matthew26:17 The first day of the festival arrived, when the Jews eat flat bread. Jesus' disciples came to him and they asked him, ‘Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal for you to eat?’" 
Matthew26:18 Jesus replied, ‘Go to a certain man in the city and say to him, “The teacher says: This is the moment that God has prepared for me. I will eat the Passover meal with my disciples in your house.” ’" 
Matthew26:19 So Jesus' disciples did what he had told them to do. They went and they prepared the Passover meal. 
Matthew26:20 When it was evening, Jesus and the 12 disciples sat down to eat the Passover meal together." 
Matthew26:21 While they were eating, Jesus said, ‘I tell you this. One of you will help my enemies to take hold of me.’" 
Matthew26:22 They were very sad about what Jesus had said. Each one of them said to Jesus, ‘ Lord, surely you do not mean me, do you?’" 
Matthew26:23 Jesus said to them ‘The man who will give me to the rulers is eating from the same dish as I am. 
Matthew26:24 The Son of Man must die in the way that the Bible says. But it will be very bad for the man who gives me to my enemies. It would be better for that man if he had not been born.’ 
Matthew26:25 Judas was the one who would give Jesus to the Jewish leaders. But he said to Jesus, ‘Teacher, surely you do not mean me, do you?’ Jesus replied to him, ‘Yes, you have said it.’" 
Matthew26:26 While Jesus and his disciples were eating, he took a loaf of bread. He thanked God for it and then he broke the bread into pieces. He gave some of it to each of them. He said, ‘Take this bread and eat it. This is my body.’" 
Matthew26:27 Then Jesus took a cup. He thanked God for the wine in the cup. Then he gave it to them and he said, ‘All of you should drink some of this wine." 
Matthew26:28 This is my blood that shows the agreement that God makes with his people. When I die, my blood will pour out of my body. In that way God will forgive many people for the wrong things that they have done. That is the agreement that God makes because of my death." 
Matthew26:29 I tell you this. I will not drink wine again until I drink it with you in my Father's kingdom. Then it will be new wine.’ 
Matthew26:30 Then Jesus and his disciples sang a song to praise God. Then they went out to the Mount of Olives. 
Matthew26:31 Then Jesus said to his disciples, ‘Tonight, all of you will turn away from me. It will happen in the way that the Bible says: God says, “I will kill the shepherd who leads the sheep. Then all the sheep will run away in different directions.” ’" 
Matthew26:32 Jesus then said, ‘But after that happens, I will become alive again. Then I will go to Galilee, and you will meet me there.’" 
Matthew26:33 Peter said to Jesus, ‘Even if everyone else runs away, I will not leave you.’" 
Matthew26:34 Jesus replied to Peter, ‘I tell you this. Even tonight, you will say three times that you do not know me. This will happen before the cockerel sings early tomorrow morning.’" 
Matthew26:35 But Peter said to Jesus, ‘I will die with you if I need to. But I will never say that I do not know you.’ All the other disciples said the same thing." 
Matthew26:36 Then Jesus and his disciples arrived at a large garden called Gethsemane. Jesus said to the disciples, ‘Sit here while I go over there to pray.’" 
Matthew26:37 Then Jesus took Peter and Zebedee's two sons, James and John, with him. He became very sad and upset." 
Matthew26:37 Then Jesus took Peter and Zebedee's two sons, James and John, with him. He became very sad and upset." 
Matthew26:37 Then Jesus took Peter and Zebedee's two sons, James and John, with him. He became very sad and upset." 
Matthew26:38 He said to them, ‘I am very sad. I feel as if I could die because I feel so sad. Wait here with me and stay awake.’" 
Matthew26:39 Jesus went a short way beyond them. He went down with his face on the ground. He prayed, ‘Father, if it is possible, please save me from this time of great pain. But Father, I do not ask you to do what I want. Do what you want to do.’" 
Matthew26:40 Jesus returned to the three disciples. Now they were sleeping. He said to Peter, ‘You men could not stay awake with me for even one hour!" 
Matthew26:41 You must stay awake and pray. God can help you, so that you will not do something wrong. You really want to do the right thing, but your bodies are weak.’" 
Matthew26:42 Jesus went away a second time and he prayed, ‘My Father, if it is not possible to save me from this time of great pain, then I want to obey you. Do what you want for me.’" 
Matthew26:43 Then Jesus returned again to Peter, James and John. He saw that they were sleeping. They could not keep their eyes open." 
Matthew26:43 Then Jesus returned again to Peter, James and John. He saw that they were sleeping. They could not keep their eyes open." 
Matthew26:43 Then Jesus returned again to Peter, James and John. He saw that they were sleeping. They could not keep their eyes open." 
Matthew26:44 So Jesus went away from them again and he prayed a third time. He said the same words to God. 
Matthew26:45 When he returned to the disciples, he said, ‘You should not still be sleeping and resting. Look! The moment has arrived! Someone will now give me, the Son of Man, to my enemies." 
Matthew26:46 Stand up, we will go now. Look! The man who will give me to my enemies is here.’" 
Matthew26:47 While Jesus was still speaking, Judas arrived. He was one of Jesus' 12 disciples. A crowd also came with him. They were carrying swords and heavy sticks. The leaders of the priests and the important Jews had sent these people with Judas." 
Matthew26:48 Judas was ready to help the Jewish leaders to take hold of Jesus. Before this, he had told them, ‘I will kiss one of the men. You must take hold of that man.’" 
Matthew26:49 Now Judas went immediately to Jesus. He said, ‘Hello, Teacher.’ Then he kissed Jesus in a friendly way." 
Matthew26:50 Jesus said to Judas, ‘My friend, now do what you have come here to do.’ Then the crowd of men came up to Jesus. They took hold of him to lead him away." 
Matthew26:51 Then one of Jesus' disciples took hold of his sword. He hit the servant of the leader of the priests with it and he cut off the servant's ear. 
Matthew26:52 Then Jesus said to the disciple, ‘Put your sword back in its place. People who use a sword to kill others will themselves die in the same way. Someone will kill them with a sword." 
Matthew26:53 You should know that I could ask my Father God to help me. He would immediately send more than 12 large groups of angels to fight for me. 
Matthew26:54 They would save me. But then the things that God has said in the Bible about this would not happen. So it must happen in this way.’ 
Matthew26:55 At that time, Jesus said to the crowd, ‘You have come out here with swords and heavy sticks to take hold of me. Do you really believe that I am a dangerous robber? Every day I was teaching people in the yard of the temple. But you did not try to take hold of me then." 
Matthew26:56 The prophets wrote long ago that all this would happen to me. Now it has become true.’ Then all of Jesus' disciples ran away and left him. 
Matthew26:57 Then those men who had taken hold of Jesus took him to Caiaphas's house. Caiaphas was the leader of the priests. The teachers of God's Law and the important Jews were meeting there together with Caiaphas. 
Matthew26:58 Peter followed Jesus into the yard of Caiaphas' house. But he did not go near Jesus. He sat down in the yard with the guards who worked in the temple. He waited to see what would happen. 
Matthew26:59 The leaders of the priests and all the Jewish leaders wanted to punish Jesus with death. So they tried to find some people who would say things against Jesus that were not true. 
Matthew26:60 Many people did come. And they said things against Jesus that were not true. But still the Jewish leaders could not find a reason to kill Jesus. Then two men stood up and they said," 
Matthew26:61 ‘We heard this man say, “I can destroy the temple and in three days I can build it again.” ’" 
Matthew26:62 Then Caiaphas stood up. He said to Jesus, ‘You must reply now to what these men have said against you. Are the things that they say true?’" 
Matthew26:63 But Jesus did not say anything. So Caiaphas said to him, ‘I use the authority of the God who lives. You must promise to tell us what is true. Are you the Messiah? Are you the Son of God?’" 
Matthew26:64 Jesus replied, ‘You have said it. And I tell all of you, soon you will see the Son of Man. He will be sitting in the most important place at the right side of the Most Powerful God. You will also see him coming to earth. He will be riding on the clouds in the sky.’" 
Matthew26:65 Then Caiaphas tore his clothes to show that he was angry. He said, ‘Jesus has spoken bad words against God. We do not need anyone else to speak against Jesus. You have heard him speak bad words against God." 
Matthew26:66 Do you think that he is guilty?’ They replied, ‘He is guilty and he deserves to die.’" 
Matthew26:67 Then some of them spat into Jesus' face. They also hit him with their fists. 
Matthew26:68 When they hit him, they said, ‘Messiah! Show us that you are a prophet. Tell us who hit you!’" 
Matthew26:69 While all these things were happening, Peter was sitting outside in the yard. One of the girls who worked there went to him and she said, ‘You were also a friend of Jesus, the man from Galilee.’" 
Matthew26:70 Peter said in front of everyone who was there, ‘That is not true. I do not know what you are talking about.’" 
Matthew26:71 Peter then went out to the gate, and another servant girl saw him. This girl said to the people who were standing there, ‘This man was a friend of Jesus, the man from Nazareth.’" 
Matthew26:72 Peter answered again very strongly. He said, ‘I promise you, I do not know that man!’" 
Matthew26:73 After a little time, other people who were standing at the gate spoke to Peter. They said, ‘We are sure that you too are one of that man's friends. We know this because you speak like people who live in Galilee.’" 
Matthew26:74 Peter said to them very strongly, ‘I tell you that I do not know that man. God will surely punish me if this is not true!’ Immediately after Peter said this, the cockerel sang." 
Matthew26:75 Then Peter remembered that Jesus had said to him, ‘Tonight you will say three times that you do not know me. You will do it before the cockerel sings.’ So Peter went out of the yard. He began to weep a lot because he was very upset." 
Matthew27:1 Early the next morning, all the leaders of the priests and the important Jews met together. They decided how to make the Roman rulers punish Jesus with death." 
Matthew27:2 They tied Jesus' hands and feet and then they took him to Pilate's house. They put him under the authority of Pilate, who was the Roman ruler." 
Matthew27:3 Judas heard that the Jewish leaders wanted the Roman rulers to kill Jesus. Judas was the man who had helped Jesus' enemies to take hold of him. Now he was very sorry about what he had done. So he took back the 30 silver coins to the leaders of the priests and the important Jews. 
Matthew27:4 He said to them, ‘I have done a bad thing. I helped you to take hold of a man who has done nothing wrong.’ They said to Judas, ‘That is not important to us. That is your problem.’" 
Matthew27:5 So Judas took the money and he threw it down on the floor in the temple. Then he went away. He used a rope to hang himself so that he died. 
Matthew27:6 The leaders of the priests picked up the coins. They said, ‘We used this money to catch and kill a man. So we cannot use this same money for the temple work.’" 
Matthew27:7 They decided to use the money to buy a field. They wanted to use the field to bury foreign people who died in the city. The field had been called ‘The Pot-maker's Field’. 
Matthew27:8 After this, it was called ‘The Field of Blood’. It is still called that even today." 
Matthew27:9 So the words that the prophet Jeremiah wrote long ago now became true. He had said, ‘Then they took the 30 silver coins. It was the amount of money the people of Israel had agreed to pay for the man." 
Matthew27:10 They used this money to buy the pot-maker's field. The Lord God had told me to do this.’ 
Matthew27:11 Now Jesus stood in front of the Roman ruler, Pilate. The ruler asked Jesus, ‘Are you the king of the Jews?’ Jesus replied, ‘You have said it.’" 
Matthew27:12 The leaders of the priests and the important Jews spoke against Jesus. They said that he had done many bad things. Jesus did not answer them. 
Matthew27:13 So Pilate said to Jesus, ‘You hear what these men are saying against you. You should reply.’" 
Matthew27:14 But Jesus did not reply to what the men were saying against him. He did not reply even to one thing. Pilate was very surprised about this. 
Matthew27:15 Each year, at the time for the Passover festival, the Roman ruler would let one person go free out of the prison. The people could choose which person should go free." 
Matthew27:16 At that time, there was a man called Barabbas in prison. Everyone knew about the bad things that he had done." 
Matthew27:17 When the crowd came together at Pilate's house, Pilate asked them, ‘Who do you want me to let go free? Should Barabbas go free? Or should it be Jesus, who is called the Messiah?’" 
Matthew27:18 Pilate knew why the Jewish leaders had brought Jesus to him. They were jealous because people liked Jesus so much. 
Matthew27:19 Pilate was sitting on his special seat as a judge. Then his wife sent a message to him. She said, ‘Do not do anything to that man. He has done nothing wrong. Last night I had a dream about him. It gave me a lot of trouble in my mind.’" 
Matthew27:20 But the leaders of the priests and the important Jews talked to the crowds of people. They said, ‘You must ask Pilate to let Barabbas go free. Then he must tell the soldiers to kill Jesus.’" 
Matthew27:21 Pilate asked the people again, ‘Which of these two men should go free?’ The people answered, ‘Barabbas.’" 
Matthew27:22 Then Pilate asked the people, ‘So what should I do with Jesus, who is called the Messiah?’ The people all shouted, ‘Take him and kill him on a cross!’" 
Matthew27:23 So Pilate said, ‘Why should I kill him? What bad things has he done?’ But the people shouted even louder, ‘Kill him on a cross!’" 
Matthew27:24 Then Pilate knew that he could not do anything to make them quiet. He thought that the people would start to fight his soldiers. So he took a bowl of water and he washed his hands in front of the people. He said, ‘It is not because of me that this man will die. You are the people who have caused it to happen.’" 
Matthew27:25 All the people answered Pilate, ‘Yes, God should punish us and our children, if we have done the wrong thing.’" 
Matthew27:26 Then Pilate let Barabbas go free, as the crowd wanted. But he said to his soldiers, ‘Beat Jesus many times with a whip. Then take him and fix him to a cross to die.’" 
Matthew27:27 So Pilate's soldiers took Jesus into the yard of the ruler's house. All the other soldiers in their group were there. 
Matthew27:28 They removed Jesus' clothes from him. They put a purple coat on him. 
Matthew27:29 They used some branches with thorns to make a crown for him. Then they put it on his head. They put a long stick in his right hand. They went down on their knees in front of him. They laughed at him and they said, ‘Hello, king of the Jews, you are great!’" 
Matthew27:30 Then the soldiers spat into Jesus' face. They took the stick and they hit Jesus on his head with it many times. 
Matthew27:31 After they had laughed at him, they took the special coat off him. They put his own clothes back on him. Then they took him out to the place where they would kill him on a cross." 
Matthew27:32 When they were going to that place, they met a man called Simon. He came from the city of Cyrene. The Roman soldiers told Simon that he must carry Jesus' cross." 
Matthew27:33 The soldiers took Jesus to the place that is called Golgotha. (Golgotha means the place of a skull.) 
Matthew27:34 They tried to give Jesus some wine to drink. They had mixed some medicine into the wine. Jesus tasted it, but he would not drink it." 
Matthew27:35 Then the soldiers fixed Jesus onto the cross. They took his clothes for themselves. They played a game with dice to find out who would receive each piece of his clothes. 
Matthew27:36 Then the soldiers sat down and they watched Jesus carefully. 
Matthew27:37 Above his head they fixed a notice. This showed the reason why they were killing him. It said, ‘This man is Jesus, the king of the Jews.’" 
Matthew27:38 Then the soldiers also fixed two robbers to crosses near to Jesus. One robber was at Jesus' right side, and one was at his left side." 
Matthew27:39 The people who walked near there insulted Jesus. They laughed at him," 
Matthew27:40 and they said, ‘You said that you could destroy the temple and then build it again in three days. If you really are the Son of God, save yourself. Come down from the cross.’" 
Matthew27:41 The leaders of the priests and the teachers of God's Law and the important Jews also laughed at him. 
Matthew27:42 They said, ‘This man saved other people, did he? But he cannot save his own life! He says that he is the king of Israel. So he should come down from the cross now. Then we will believe in him." 
Matthew27:43 He says that he trusts in God. So if God wants him, God should save him now. He did say that he is the Son of God.’" 
Matthew27:44 Then the two robbers who were on the crosses next to Jesus also insulted him. 
Matthew27:45 It was now midday. The whole country became dark for three hours. 
Matthew27:46 At about three o'clock in the afternoon, Jesus shouted loudly, ‘Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?’ That means, ‘My God, my God, why have you left me alone?’" 
Matthew27:47 Some people who were standing there heard him. They said to each other, ‘He is shouting to Elijah.’" 
Matthew27:48 One of these people ran quickly and he brought a piece of cloth. He poured bitter wine on it, and he put it on the end of a stick. He lifted it up to Jesus so that he could drink the wine from it." 
Matthew27:49 The other people said, ‘Wait! Now we will see if Elijah comes to save him.’" 
Matthew27:50 Then Jesus shouted again with a loud voice, and after that he died." 
Matthew27:51 At that moment, the curtain inside the temple tore completely into two parts. It tore from the top down to the bottom. The ground shook and rocks broke into pieces." 
Matthew27:52 Places in the ground where people had buried dead bodies opened up. Many of God's people who had died now became alive again. 
Matthew27:53 (Later, after Jesus became alive again, those people came up out of those places. They went into God's city, Jerusalem. Many people saw them there.)" 
Matthew27:54 The army captain and his soldiers had been watching Jesus. They saw the ground shaking. They saw everything that had happened. They were very frightened and they said, ‘It must be true! This man really was the Son of God.’" 
Matthew27:55 There were also many women there. They were standing a long way away and they were watching these events. They had come with Jesus from Galilee to help him. 
Matthew27:56 Mary from Magdala was there. Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Mary the mother of Zebedee's sons, were also there." 
Matthew27:56 Mary from Magdala was there. Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Mary the mother of Zebedee's sons, were also there." 
Matthew27:57 That evening a rich man who came from a town called Arimathea went to Pilate. The man's name was Joseph, and he had become a disciple of Jesus." 
Matthew27:58 Joseph asked Pilate for the dead body of Jesus. Pilate told his soldiers to give Jesus' body to Joseph. 
Matthew27:59 So Joseph took Jesus' body. He put a clean piece of linen cloth around it. 
Matthew27:60 Then he put the body into a new grave hole in a rock. He had prepared the grave for his own body when he died. After he put Jesus' body there, he rolled a very big stone across the front of the hole to shut it. Then he went away." 
Matthew27:61 Mary from Magdala and Mary the mother of James were there. They were sitting where they could see the place where Joseph had put Jesus' body. 
Matthew27:61 Mary from Magdala and Mary the mother of James were there. They were sitting where they could see the place where Joseph had put Jesus' body. 
Matthew27:62 The next day was the Jewish day of rest. The leaders of the priests and the Pharisees met together with Pilate. 
Matthew27:63 They said to him, ‘Sir, that man told lies. When he was still living, he said, “Three days after I die, I will become alive again.” We know that is what he said." 
Matthew27:64 So you should tell some soldiers to watch the place where Joseph put Jesus' body. They should watch there for the next three days. Then his disciples cannot come to take his body away secretly. If they did that, then they could say to the people, “God has caused Jesus to become alive again.” This will be worse than the lies that Jesus told when he was alive.’" 
Matthew27:65 Pilate said to them, ‘You may take a group of soldiers to watch the place. Let them make sure that nobody can open it.’" 
Matthew27:66 So the Jewish leaders and the soldiers went to the place where Joseph had put Jesus' body. They fixed the big stone that closed the front of the hole with a seal. Then they would know if someone moved it. The soldiers stayed there to watch the place very carefully. 
Matthew28:1 After the day of rest had finished, Mary from Magdala and Mary the mother of James got up early. It was dawn on the first day of the week. They went to the grave hole in the rock where Joseph had put the dead body of Jesus." 
Matthew28:1 After the day of rest had finished, Mary from Magdala and Mary the mother of James got up early. It was dawn on the first day of the week. They went to the grave hole in the rock where Joseph had put the dead body of Jesus." 
Matthew28:2 At that moment, there was a strong earthquake. One of the Lord God's angels came down from heaven. He rolled the big stone away from the hole in the rock. Then he sat on top of it." 
Matthew28:3 The angel shone brightly like lightning. His clothes were very white like snow. 
Matthew28:4 The soldiers who were carefully watching that place were very frightened. They suddenly fell down on the ground as if they were dead. 
Matthew28:5 Then the angel said to the women, ‘Do not be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus. The soldiers killed him on a cross." 
Matthew28:6 But he is not here. He has become alive again. That is what he said would happen. Come here. You can see the place where he was lying. 
Matthew28:7 Then you must go quickly to his disciples. Tell them, “Jesus is alive again and he is going to Galilee. You must also go there and you will see him.” That is the message that I have brought for you.’" 
Matthew28:8 So the women went away quickly from the grave in the rock. They were very frightened, but they were also very happy. They ran to tell the disciples the angel's message." 
Matthew28:9 At that moment, Jesus met the two women and he said, ‘Hello.’ They went near to him. They held on to his feet and they worshipped him." 
Matthew28:10 Then Jesus said to them, ‘Do not be afraid. Go and tell my disciples to go to Galilee. They will see me there.’" 
Matthew28:11 The women went to tell the disciples Jesus' message. At the same time, some of the soldiers who had been guards at the rock also went into the city. The soldiers told the leaders of the priests everything that had happened." 
Matthew28:12 The leaders of the priests and the important Jews met together. They decided what they should do now. They gave the soldiers a lot of money as a bribe. 
Matthew28:13 They said to the soldiers, ‘When people ask you, you must say this: “Jesus' disciples came to the rock in the night and they took his body away. We were asleep when they did this.”" 
Matthew28:14 If the Roman ruler Pilate hears about this, we will explain things to him. Do not be afraid. He will not punish you.’" 
Matthew28:15 The soldiers took the money. They told people what the Jewish leaders told them to say. Many Jews heard the soldiers' story. Even today they still believe that it happened like that. 
Matthew28:16 After this, the 11 disciples went to Galilee. They went to the mountain where Jesus had told them to go." 
Matthew28:17 When they arrived at the place, they saw Jesus. Then they went down on their knees and they worshipped him. But some of the disciples were not sure that it really was Jesus." 
Matthew28:18 Jesus went near to them and he said to them, ‘God has given me authority over everyone and everything. I have all authority in heaven and in this world." 
Matthew28:19 So you must go to people in every country of the world. Teach them how to become my disciples. Baptize them by the authority of God the Father, his Son and the Holy Spirit." 
Matthew28:20 Teach them to obey everything that I have taught you. You can be sure that I will be with you always. I will be with you until the end of time.’ 
Mark1:1 This is the start of the good news about Jesus Christ. He is the Son of God. 
Mark1:2 The prophet Isaiah wrote this message from God in his book: ‘Listen! I will send someone to prepare a way for you. He will tell people my message. 
Mark1:3 His voice will shout in the wilderness: “The Lord will come soon, so prepare a way for him. Make the paths straight for him.” ’" 
Mark1:4 So John went to a place in the wilderness. He baptized people and he spoke a message from God. John said to people, ‘You have done many wrong things. You must turn away from them and change how you live. Then God will forgive you and I will baptize you.’" 
Mark1:4 So John went to a place in the wilderness. He baptized people and he spoke a message from God. John said to people, ‘You have done many wrong things. You must turn away from them and change how you live. Then God will forgive you and I will baptize you.’" 
Mark1:5 Many people who lived in Jerusalem and in the country of Judea went to listen to John. The people agreed that they had done wrong things. Then John baptized them in the Jordan River. 
Mark1:5 Many people who lived in Jerusalem and in the country of Judea went to listen to John. The people agreed that they had done wrong things. Then John baptized them in the Jordan River. 
Mark1:5 Many people who lived in Jerusalem and in the country of Judea went to listen to John. The people agreed that they had done wrong things. Then John baptized them in the Jordan River. 
Mark1:6 John wore clothes made from the hair of a camel. He also wore a belt made from leather. His usual food was locusts and honey from the wilderness. 
Mark1:6 John wore clothes made from the hair of a camel. He also wore a belt made from leather. His usual food was locusts and honey from the wilderness. 
Mark1:7 John said to the people, ‘Another person will come soon. He is much greater than I am. I am not good enough even to undo his shoes for him." 
Mark1:7 John said to the people, ‘Another person will come soon. He is much greater than I am. I am not good enough even to undo his shoes for him." 
Mark1:8 I have baptized you with water. But this man will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.’ 
Mark1:9 Soon after this, Jesus came from the town of Nazareth in Galilee. John baptized him in the Jordan River." 
Mark1:9 Soon after this, Jesus came from the town of Nazareth in Galilee. John baptized him in the Jordan River." 
Mark1:10 While Jesus was coming up out of the water, he saw the skies open. The Holy Spirit came down like a dove and he stayed on Jesus." 
Mark1:11 A voice spoke from heaven: ‘You are my Son and I love you. You make me very happy.’ 
Mark1:12 Immediately, the Holy Spirit sent Jesus out into the wilderness." 
Mark1:13 Jesus was in the wilderness for 40 days. During this time, Satan tried to cause him to do wrong things. There were wild animals with Jesus in the wilderness. God's angels took care of him." 
Mark1:14 Later, John was in prison. At that time, Jesus went to Galilee. He told people the good news about God." 
Mark1:14 Later, John was in prison. At that time, Jesus went to Galilee. He told people the good news about God." 
Mark1:15 Jesus said, ‘Now is the time when the kingdom of God has come very near. You have done many wrong things. Turn away from them and change the way that you live. Believe God's good news.’" 
Mark1:16 One day, Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee. He saw two brothers called Simon and Andrew. Their job was to catch fish. They were throwing their nets into the lake to catch fish." 
Mark1:17 Jesus said to them, ‘Come with me and be my disciples. Then I will teach you how to catch people, instead of fish.’" 
Mark1:18 Simon and Andrew immediately put down their nets and they went with Jesus. 
Mark1:19 Jesus continued to walk along the shore. Soon he saw two more men who were brothers. They were called James and John. They were the sons of Zebedee. They were in their boat and they were mending their nets. 
Mark1:19 Jesus continued to walk along the shore. Soon he saw two more men who were brothers. They were called James and John. They were the sons of Zebedee. They were in their boat and they were mending their nets. 
Mark1:19 Jesus continued to walk along the shore. Soon he saw two more men who were brothers. They were called James and John. They were the sons of Zebedee. They were in their boat and they were mending their nets. 
Mark1:20 Immediately, Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father and his workers in the boat, and they went with Jesus to be his disciples." 
Mark1:21 Then Jesus and those disciples went into a town called Capernaum. On the next Jewish day of rest, Jesus went into the Jews' meeting place. He began to teach the people there." 
Mark1:22 The people were very surprised at the things that Jesus taught them. When he taught them, he showed his authority. That was not like the way that the teachers of God's Law taught people." 
Mark1:23 In the meeting place, there was a man who had a bad spirit. The bad spirit caused the man to shout." 
Mark1:24 He said, ‘Jesus from Nazareth, leave us alone! Do not kill us! I know who you are. You are the Holy One and God has sent you.’" 
Mark1:25 Jesus said to the bad spirit, ‘Be quiet! Come out of the man!’" 
Mark1:26 The bad spirit caused the man to shake and to fall down. It shouted loudly and then it came out of the man. 
Mark1:27 All the people were very surprised and they said to each other, ‘This is very strange. This man Jesus is teaching us something new and he speaks with authority. He can tell bad spirits what they must do. Then they do it.’" 
Mark1:28 Immediately, people began to hear the news about Jesus. Everywhere in Galilee, people were talking about him." 
Mark1:29 Then Jesus left the meeting place. He took James and John with him to the home of Simon and Andrew. 
Mark1:29 Then Jesus left the meeting place. He took James and John with him to the home of Simon and Andrew. 
Mark1:29 Then Jesus left the meeting place. He took James and John with him to the home of Simon and Andrew. 
Mark1:30 The mother of Simon's wife was ill in bed. Her body was very hot. Immediately, Simon and Andrew told Jesus about her." 
Mark1:31 So Jesus went to her and he held her hand. Then he helped her to sit up and she was well again. Immediately she prepared food for her visitors. 
Mark1:32 That evening, when the sun had gone down, people in the town came to Jesus. They brought to him everyone who was ill. And they brought people with bad spirits in them. All these people were standing together near the door of the house." 
Mark1:33  
Mark1:34 Jesus caused many sick people to become well. They had many different kinds of illness. He also caused many bad spirits to leave people. The spirits knew who Jesus was. So he did not let them speak. 
Mark1:35 Jesus got up very early the next morning, while it was still dark. He went to a place where he could be alone. He prayed there." 
Mark1:36 Simon and the other disciples went to look for him. 
Mark1:37 When they found him, they said, ‘Everyone is looking for you.’" 
Mark1:38 Jesus replied, ‘We should go to other towns near here. Then I can teach the people there too. That is the reason why I came here.’" 
Mark1:39 So Jesus travelled everywhere in Galilee. He taught the people in the Jewish meeting places. He caused bad spirits to come out of people. 
Mark1:40 One day, a man who had a bad skin disease came to Jesus. He went down on his knees in front of Jesus. The man said. ‘If you want, you can make me well. Please do it.’" 
Mark1:41 Jesus felt very sorry for the man. He put out his hand towards the man and he touched him. Jesus said, ‘I do want to help you. Be clean again!’" 
Mark1:42 Immediately, the disease left the man." 
Mark1:43 Jesus sent the man away immediately and he spoke to the man with strong words. 
Mark1:44 He said, ‘Listen to me. Do not tell anyone about this. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest. Take him a gift for God. Moses' law tells you what you should give when you are clean again after this kind of disease. This will show everyone that you are now well again.’" 
Mark1:45 But instead, the man went away and he talked to everybody. He told people what had happened to him. Because he did this, it became too difficult for Jesus to go into the towns. So Jesus stayed in places in the wilderness. But people came from all the places around there to see him." 
Mark2:1 Several days after that, Jesus returned to Capernaum. People heard the news that he had come back to his home." 
Mark2:2 Many people came into the house. The house was so full that there was no room, even outside the door. Jesus was teaching people about the good news." 
Mark2:3 Four men came. They were carrying another man on a mat. The man could not move his legs. 
Mark2:4 They could not reach Jesus because of the crowd. So they made a hole in the roof above the place where Jesus was. They helped the man to go down through the hole. He was still lying on his mat. 
Mark2:5 Jesus saw the man and his friends. He knew that they believed in him. So he said to the man who could not walk, ‘My friend, I forgive you for your sins.’" 
Mark2:6 But some teachers of God's Law were sitting there. They thought about the words that Jesus had spoken to the man. 
Mark2:7 They thought, ‘This man Jesus should not have said that. He is speaking as if he is God. Only God can forgive people for their sins.’" 
Mark2:8 Immediately, Jesus knew in his spirit what the teachers were thinking. He said to them, ‘You should not think these things." 
Mark2:9 I said to this man who cannot walk, “I forgive you for your sins.” Instead, I could have said to him, “Stand up! Pick up your mat and walk.” Which of those is easier for me to say?" 
Mark2:10 But I want you to know this. I, the Son of Man, have authority on earth to forgive people for their sins.’ Then he said to the man who could not walk," 
Mark2:11 ‘I am saying to you, stand up! Pick up your mat and go home.’" 
Mark2:12 Immediately, the man stood up. He picked up his mat and he walked out of the house. Everyone watched him do this. The people were very surprised. They praised God and they said, ‘God is great. We have never seen anything like this before.’" 
Mark2:13 Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee again. A large crowd came to him, and he taught them." 
Mark2:14 While Jesus was walking along, he saw a man called Levi, the son of Alphaeus. Levi's job was to take taxes from people. He was sitting in his office. Jesus said to him, ‘Come with me and be my disciple.’ Levi stood up and he went with Jesus." 
Mark2:15 Then Jesus went to eat a meal at Levi's house. Many people followed Jesus and they ate there with him and with his disciples. Many of these were bad people and also men who took taxes. 
Mark2:16 Some teachers of God's Law who were Pharisees saw what was happening. They said to Jesus' disciples, ‘He is eating with bad people and men who take taxes. That is not right.’" 
Mark2:17 Jesus heard what these people were saying. He said to them, ‘People who are well do not need a doctor. It is people who are ill that need a doctor. Some people think that they always obey God. I did not come to help people like that. But some people know that they have done wrong things. I am asking those people to come to me for help.’" 
Mark2:18 At this time, the disciples of John the Baptist and the disciples of the Pharisees were fasting. Some people came to Jesus and they asked him this question: ‘The disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees often fast for a time. So why do your disciples never do that?’" 
Mark2:18 At this time, the disciples of John the Baptist and the disciples of the Pharisees were fasting. Some people came to Jesus and they asked him this question: ‘The disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees often fast for a time. So why do your disciples never do that?’" 
Mark2:19 Jesus answered them, ‘When a man marries, his friends cannot refuse to eat. They cannot fast while he is with them." 
Mark2:20 But there will be a time when people will take the man away from his friends. At that time his friends will fast.’ 
Mark2:21 Then Jesus said, ‘Nobody uses a piece of new cloth to mend an old coat. If he does, the new cloth will cause the old cloth to tear again. It will make a bigger hole than before." 
Mark2:22 And nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does that, the new wine will tear the old wineskins. He will lose the wine and the wineskins will be useless. Instead, you must put new wine into new wineskins.’" 
Mark2:23 On one Jewish day of rest, Jesus and his disciples were walking through some fields where wheat was growing. While they were walking along, his disciples picked some grains of wheat." 
Mark2:24 Some Pharisees said to Jesus, ‘Look at what your disciples are doing. They should not do that on our day of rest. It is against God's Law.’" 
Mark2:25 Jesus replied, ‘You have certainly read about what David did one day. He and the men who were with him were very hungry. They needed food to eat." 
Mark2:26 David went into the temple. He ate the special bread that was there. He gave some of it to his men to eat as well. This happened during the time that Abiathar was the leader of the priests. It is against God's Law for anyone except the priests to eat that special bread.’ 
Mark2:27 Then Jesus said to the Pharisees, ‘God wanted to help people. So he made a day for them when they should rest. He did not make people so that they could keep laws about the day of rest." 
Mark2:28 So you should know that the Son of Man has authority over rules about the day of rest.’ 
Mark3:1 Another time, Jesus went into the Jewish meeting place. A man was there. His hand was very small and weak, so he could not use it." 
Mark3:2 Some Pharisees were watching Jesus carefully. They wanted to find a reason to say that Jesus was doing wrong things. It was the Jewish day of rest. So the Pharisees watched Jesus to see if he would make the man well on this day. 
Mark3:3 Jesus said to the man, ‘Stand here in front of everyone.’" 
Mark3:4 Then Jesus said to the Pharisees, ‘Is it right for us to do good things on our day of rest? Or should we do bad things? Should we save a person's life? Or should we kill people?’ Nobody said anything." 
Mark3:5 Jesus looked round at everybody. He felt angry with them. He was sad because they did not want to learn. Then he said to the man, ‘Lift up your hand.’ The man lifted his hand and it became well. He could use it again." 
Mark3:6 The Pharisees left the meeting place immediately. They went to meet with some other people who were friends with the ruler, Herod. The Pharisees talked with them about how they could kill Jesus." 
Mark3:7 Jesus left that place and he went away to Lake Galilee with his disciples. A large crowd of people from Galilee followed them. 
Mark3:8 Many people also came to him from Judea, from Jerusalem and from a region of Israel called Idumea. They also came from places on the other side of the Jordan River, and from the towns called Tyre and Sidon. Many people had heard about the things that Jesus was doing. That is why all these people came to him." 
Mark3:8 Many people also came to him from Judea, from Jerusalem and from a region of Israel called Idumea. They also came from places on the other side of the Jordan River, and from the towns called Tyre and Sidon. Many people had heard about the things that Jesus was doing. That is why all these people came to him." 
Mark3:9 The crowd was very large. So Jesus asked his disciples to prepare a small boat for him. They did this so that the people would not push against him. 
Mark3:10 Sick people were pushing to the front, because they were trying to touch Jesus. They knew that he had made many people well." 
Mark3:11 Often, a person with a bad spirit saw Jesus. Then, the spirit caused the person to fall down on the ground in front of Jesus. The spirit caused that person to shout out, ‘You are the Son of God.’" 
Mark3:12 Jesus often had to say to the bad spirits, ‘You must not tell anyone who I am.’" 
Mark3:13 Jesus went up a mountain. He chose some men and he asked them to go there with him. So they met together with him there. 
Mark3:14 He chose a group of 12 men. He called them his apostles. He wanted them to be with him. And he would send them to teach people about God. 
Mark3:15 Jesus gave these men authority to cause bad spirits to leave people. 
Mark3:16 These are the names of the 12 apostles: Simon, (Jesus called him Peter)." 
Mark3:17 James and John who were the sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them ‘Boanerges’. It means ‘men who are like thunder ’. 
Mark3:17 James and John who were the sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them ‘Boanerges’. It means ‘men who are like thunder ’. 
Mark3:17 James and John who were the sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them ‘Boanerges’. It means ‘men who are like thunder ’. 
Mark3:18 Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew (he was also called Levi), Thomas, James, who was the son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot" 
Mark3:18 Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew (he was also called Levi), Thomas, James, who was the son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot" 
Mark3:18 Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew (he was also called Levi), Thomas, James, who was the son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot" 
Mark3:19 and Judas Iscariot, who later gave Jesus to his enemies." 
Mark3:20 Then Jesus went into a house. A crowd of people came together there again. There were so many people that Jesus and his disciples were not even able to eat. 
Mark3:21 People told his family what was happening. So they went to take him away with them. They did this because many people were saying that Jesus was crazy. 
Mark3:22 Some teachers of God's Law came from Jerusalem. They said, ‘Jesus has a bad spirit called Beelzebul in him. That is Satan, the one who rules all the demons. This man can send demons out of people because Satan gives him authority.’" 
Mark3:23 So Jesus spoke to the teachers of God's Law. He used stories to talk to them. He said, ‘Come here and listen to me. Satan would not fight against himself!" 
Mark3:24 If armies in the same country start to fight each other, then they will destroy their own country." 
Mark3:25 And if the people in one family start to fight against each other, they will destroy their own family." 
Mark3:26 So Satan would not attack himself. If he did that, he would destroy his own power. That would be the end of him." 
Mark3:27 Nobody can easily go into the house of a strong man to rob him. To do that, he must first tie up the strong man. Then he can take away all that man's valuable things." 
Mark3:28 I tell you this. God can forgive all the wrong things that people do. He can also forgive people who say bad things against him. 
Mark3:29 But God will never forgive people who say bad things against the Holy Spirit. Those people will always be guilty of a terrible sin.’ 
Mark3:30 Jesus said that to the teachers of God's Law because they had said, ‘Jesus has a bad spirit in him.’" 
Mark3:31 Then Jesus' mother and his brothers arrived and they stood outside the house. They sent someone into the house with a message. They wanted Jesus to come out to speak to them. 
Mark3:32 A crowd of people was sitting around Jesus. Somebody said to him, ‘Look! Your mother and brothers are outside. They are looking for you.’" 
Mark3:33 Jesus replied, ‘I will tell you who my mother and my brothers really are.’" 
Mark3:34 Then he looked at the people who were sitting around him. He said, ‘Look! Here are my mother and my brothers!" 
Mark3:35 My brothers and sisters and my mother are the people who do what God wants.’ 
Mark4:1 Again, Jesus began to teach people near the edge of Lake Galilee. A large crowd came together to listen to him. So he went and sat in a boat that was on the water. The crowd of people stayed on the shore." 
Mark4:2 Jesus used stories to teach them many things. He said to them," 
Mark4:3 ‘Listen to me! A farmer went out to plant seeds in his field. 
Mark4:4 While he was throwing the seeds, some of them fell on the path. The birds came and they ate those seeds." 
Mark4:5 Other seeds fell on ground which had rocks. There was not much soil in that place. The seeds quickly began to grow, because the soil was not deep." 
Mark4:6 But when the sun rose, it burned the young plants. They soon died because they had not grown down well into the soil." 
Mark4:7 Other seeds fell among thorn bushes. Those bushes grew up with the young plants. They stopped the seeds from growing into strong plants. So the plants could not make any new seeds. 
Mark4:8 But some seeds fell on good soil. Good strong plants grew from these seeds. Some plants made 30 new seeds. Some plants made 60 new seeds. And some plants made 100 new seeds.’ 
Mark4:9 Then Jesus said, ‘You have ears, so listen well to what I say!’" 
Mark4:10 When Jesus was alone, the 12 apostles and other people who were there asked him about the stories." 
Mark4:11 Jesus said to them, ‘God has let you understand how he rules in the lives of his people. But these other people only listen to stories." 
Mark4:12 This is so that: They are always looking but they do not see clearly. They are always listening but they do not understand. If they did understand, they would turn to God. Then God would forgive them.’" 
Mark4:13 Then Jesus said to them, ‘You should understand this story. If you do not understand it, you will not understand any of my stories." 
Mark4:14 The seeds are like the message from God. The farmer is like a person who tells people about that message. 
Mark4:15 Some seeds fell on the path. That is like some people who listen to the message. But then Satan comes and he quickly takes the message away from their minds. 
Mark4:16 Some of the seeds fell on soil with rocks in it. This is also like some people who hear God's message. These people are happy to believe it for a time. 
Mark4:17 But they are like plants that have not put roots down into the soil. So they only believe for a short time. They may have problems. Or because they obey God's message, other people may do bad things to them. When that happens, these people soon stop believing." 
Mark4:18 Some seeds fell among thorn bushes. This is like some other people who hear God's message. 
Mark4:19 But they have many troubles in their minds. They think that more money and other valuable things will make them happy. So they do not let God's message change them. They are like plants that do not grow well and make new seeds. 
Mark4:20 But some seeds fell on good soil. This is like other people who listen to the message from God. They understand the message and they believe it. These people are like good plants that grow well. From one seed, some good plants make 30 new seeds. Other good plants make 60 new seeds, and some good plants make 100 new seeds.’" 
Mark4:21 Then Jesus said to his disciples, ‘Nobody brings a lamp into a house and puts it under a pot or under a bed. You do not do that. You put it up on a high place." 
Mark4:22 God hides some things now. But there will be a time when people will see them. God keeps some things secret now. But there will be a time when people will understand those things.’ 
Mark4:23 Then Jesus said, ‘You have ears, so listen well to what I say.’" 
Mark4:24 Jesus then said, ‘Think carefully about the things that you hear. God will give to you in the same way that you give to other people. And you will receive even more." 
Mark4:25 A person who has received some things will receive more things. Some other people have nothing. They will lose even the little bit that they do have.’ 
Mark4:26 Then Jesus said, ‘I will tell you a story about the kingdom of God. It is like this: A man throws seeds in his field." 
Mark4:27 Then he sleeps each night and he wakes up each day. The seeds start to grow into plants. They continue to grow. The man does not know how this happens. 
Mark4:28 The soil causes the plants to grow. The leaves of the plant grow first. Then the flowers appear. Then the plant makes new seeds. 
Mark4:29 When the new seeds have completely grown, the man will cut down the plants. It is time for him to take the seeds to use for food.’" 
Mark4:30 Jesus said, ‘I will tell you another story about the kingdom of God. This story shows what the kingdom of God is like." 
Mark4:31 It is like this: A man takes a seed of the plant called mustard. He plants it in the soil. It is smaller than any other seed that people plant in the soil. 
Mark4:32 But when it starts to grow, it becomes bigger than the largest bush. It will have big branches. Then the birds will come and they will live there. They will build their nests in the shade of the branches.’" 
Mark4:33 Jesus taught God's message to the people. He used many stories like these. He told the people as much as they could understand. 
Mark4:34 He always used stories to teach the people. Then he explained everything to his own disciples when he was alone with them. 
Mark4:35 On that same day, in the evening, Jesus said to his disciples, ‘We should go across the lake to the other side.’" 
Mark4:36 So they went and left the crowd behind. Jesus was already in the boat. So the disciples took him across the lake. Some other boats also went with them. 
Mark4:37 Then a strong wind began to blow across the lake. Water began to go into the boat and fill it. Soon the boat was almost going under the water. 
Mark4:38 Jesus was in a comfortable place at the back of the boat. He was sleeping. The disciples woke him. They said to him, ‘Teacher, we will die here in the water! Please do something!’" 
Mark4:39 Jesus woke up and he spoke strongly to the wind and to the water. He said, ‘Be quiet! Stop!’ Then the wind stopped and the water became quiet again." 
Mark4:40 Jesus said to his disciples, ‘You should not be afraid like that. You should trust me.’" 
Mark4:41 But the disciples became very afraid. They said to each other, ‘Who is this man? Even the wind and the water obey him.’" 
Mark5:1 Jesus and his disciples arrived at the other side of the lake. They came to a place where the Gerasene people lived. 
Mark5:2 Jesus got out of the boat. Immediately, a man with a bad spirit in him came to him. This man lived outside, among some graves. He was very strong and nobody could hold him. People often put chains around his hands and his feet to hold him. They wanted to keep him in a safe place. But he broke the chains that held him." 
Mark5:3  
Mark5:4  
Mark5:5 The man often walked in the place where the graves were and on the hills. Every day and every night, he screamed and he used stones to cut himself." 
Mark5:6 The man saw Jesus a long way away and he ran to meet him. He went down on his knees in front of Jesus. 
Mark5:7 He screamed loudly, ‘Jesus, you are the Son of the Most High God! What are you doing here? Please, promise God that you will not hurt me.’" 
Mark5:8 He said that because Jesus had already said to the bad spirit, ‘Come out of this man.’" 
Mark5:9 Then Jesus asked the man, ‘What is your name?’ The man replied, ‘My name is Army because we are many bad spirits here.’" 
Mark5:10 He said to Jesus many times, ‘Please do not send these bad spirits away from this place.’" 
Mark5:11 A large group of pigs was eating on the hill. 
Mark5:12 The bad spirits said, ‘Jesus, send us to the pigs. Let us go into them.’" 
Mark5:13 Jesus let them do this. So the bad spirits came out of the man and they went into the pigs. There were about 2,000 pigs. They rushed together down the hill into the lake. All the pigs died there in the water." 
Mark5:14 The men who were taking care of these pigs ran away. They told the people in the town and in the villages what had happened. Then those people came to see the place for themselves. 
Mark5:15 They came to Jesus. They saw the man that the army of bad spirits had ruled. He was now sitting there quietly. He was wearing clothes and his mind was well again. When the people saw this, they were afraid." 
Mark5:16 The people who had seen this told other people what had happened to the man. They also explained what happened to the pigs. 
Mark5:17 Then the people who lived there said to Jesus, ‘Please go away. Leave this place.’" 
Mark5:18 Jesus got back into the boat. The man that the bad spirits had ruled spoke to him. He told Jesus that he wanted to go with him. 
Mark5:19 But Jesus did not let him do this. Jesus said to him, ‘Go to your home and to your friends. Tell them what the Lord has done for you. Tell them how he has been kind to you.’" 
Mark5:20 So the man went away. He began to speak to many people in the region called ‘The ten cities’. He told them about the great things that Jesus had done for him. All the people were very surprised. 
Mark5:21 Jesus returned to the other side of the lake in the boat. While he was beside the lake, a large crowd came to him." 
Mark5:22 A man called Jairus came to Jesus. He was a leader at the Jewish meeting place. When he saw Jesus, he went down on his knees in front of him." 
Mark5:23 He said, ‘My little daughter is very ill and she will die very soon. Please come to my house and put your hands on her. Then she will be well and she will live.’" 
Mark5:24 So Jesus went with Jairus. A large crowd followed Jesus. The people were pushing against him. 
Mark5:25 There was a sick woman who had lost blood for 12 years. 
Mark5:26 She had paid many doctors to help her to become well. But they could not do anything. Now she had spent all her money. She had not become well. Instead, she was now worse." 
Mark5:27 People had told her about Jesus. So she came in the crowd behind him and she touched his coat. 
Mark5:28 She thought, ‘Even if I can only touch his clothes, I will become well again.’" 
Mark5:29 As soon as she touched Jesus' coat, the blood stopped. She felt in her body that her illness was better. She knew that she was well again." 
Mark5:30 Jesus knew immediately that something powerful had gone out from him. So he turned around in the crowd and he asked, ‘Who touched my clothes?’" 
Mark5:31 His disciples replied, ‘You can see that the crowd is pushing against you. So you cannot ask who touched you!’" 
Mark5:32 But Jesus looked around him. He wanted to see who had touched him. 
Mark5:33 The woman knew what had happened to her. She felt very afraid. But she came to Jesus, and she went down on her knees in front of him. Then she told him everything that had happened to her." 
Mark5:34 Jesus said to her, ‘Young woman, you are now well again because you believed in me. Have peace in your mind. Go now and have no more pain.’" 
Mark5:35 While Jesus was still speaking, some men arrived from the house of Jairus. He was the leader at the Jews' meeting place. The men said to Jairus, ‘Your daughter is dead. Do not ask any longer for the teacher to come.’" 
Mark5:36 Jesus heard what the men said to Jairus. But Jesus said to him, ‘Do not be afraid. Instead, believe.’" 
Mark5:37 Jesus took only Peter, James and James's brother, John, with him. He would not let anyone else go with him." 
Mark5:37 Jesus took only Peter, James and James's brother, John, with him. He would not let anyone else go with him." 
Mark5:37 Jesus took only Peter, James and James's brother, John, with him. He would not let anyone else go with him." 
Mark5:38 Then they came to Jairus's house. Jesus saw that there were many people there. They were crying and they were making a loud noise. 
Mark5:39 Jesus went into the house and he said to the people, ‘You should not be weeping and making a noise. The child is not dead. She is asleep.’" 
Mark5:40 The people laughed at Jesus when he said that. Then Jesus sent them all out of the house. He went into the place where the child was lying. The child's father and mother went with him. He also took Peter, James and John with him."

Mark5:41 Then Jesus held the girl's hand. He said to her, ‘Talitha koum.’ This means, ‘Little girl, I say to you, stand up!’" 
Mark5:42 The girl stood up immediately and she walked about. She was 12 years old. The people there were very surprised. 
Mark5:43 Jesus said to them, ‘You must not tell anyone what has happened here. Now give the girl something to eat.’" 
Mark6:1 Jesus left that place. He went to his own town. His disciples went with him. 
Mark6:2 When it was the Jewish day of rest, Jesus went to their meeting place. He began to teach the people who were there. Many of them were surprised about the things that he was saying to them. They said to each other, ‘How did this man learn all these things? How did he become so wise? How does he do all these powerful things?" 
Mark6:3 We know he is an ordinary man. He is the carpenter. He is the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joses, Simon and Judas. His sisters live here in the town among us. We know that is true.’ So the people there did not respect Jesus." 
Mark6:3 We know he is an ordinary man. He is the carpenter. He is the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joses, Simon and Judas. His sisters live here in the town among us. We know that is true.’ So the people there did not respect Jesus." 
Mark6:4 Then Jesus said to them, ‘If a man is a prophet from God, people everywhere will respect him. But the people in his own town will not accept him. Even his own people and his own family will not respect him.’" 
Mark6:5 Jesus could not do any powerful miracles in his own town. But he did put his hands on a few sick people and he made them well. 
Mark6:6 Jesus was very surprised because the people there would not believe in him. Then he went to visit the other villages that were near there. He was teaching the people who lived in those villages. 
Mark6:7 Jesus asked his 12 apostles to come to him. He started to send them out to tell God's message to people. He sent them out two by two and he gave them authority over bad spirits. 
Mark6:8 Jesus told the disciples, ‘Only take a stick for the journey, nothing else. Do not take any bread or a bag. Do not take any money in your pockets." 
Mark6:9 Wear shoes but do not take extra clothes. 
Mark6:10 In each town, stay at the first house that you go into. Continue to stay there until you leave that town." 
Mark6:11 Perhaps you might go to a town where the people do not accept you. They will not listen to you. So you should leave that town. Clean that town's dirt off your feet. Then it will be clear that they have done something wrong.’ 
Mark6:12 So the 12 apostles went out. They told people that they must change the way that they live. They must stop doing wrong things. 
Mark6:13 The disciples caused many bad spirits to come out of people. They also put olive oil on many sick people, and the sick people became well." 
Mark6:14 People were talking about Jesus. King Herod heard about what was happening. Some people were saying that Jesus was really John the Baptist. They said, ‘John died, but now he has become alive again. That is why this man can do very powerful miracles.’" 
Mark6:14 People were talking about Jesus. King Herod heard about what was happening. Some people were saying that Jesus was really John the Baptist. They said, ‘John died, but now he has become alive again. That is why this man can do very powerful miracles.’" 
Mark6:15 Other people said, ‘This man is Elijah.’ And some other people said, ‘He is a prophet. He is like one of God's prophets who lived a long time ago.’" 
Mark6:16 But when someone told Herod, he said, ‘This man is surely John. I sent a soldier to cut off his head. But he has become alive again!’" 
Mark6:16 But when someone told Herod, he said, ‘This man is surely John. I sent a soldier to cut off his head. But he has become alive again!’" 
Mark6:17 Herod himself had sent his soldiers to take hold of John. He had said to his soldiers, ‘Tie his hands and feet and put him in prison.’ Herod had done that because of his wife, Herodias. She had been the wife of Herod's brother, Philip. But Herod had married her."

Mark6:18 John had said to him, ‘Herodias is your brother's wife. It is not right for you to have her as your wife.’"

Mark6:20 because Herod was afraid of John. Herod knew that John was a good man and a servant of God. So Herod kept John safe. He liked to listen to the things that John said. But he did not know what to think about those things.

Mark6:21 One day, Herodias had her chance. It was Herod's birthday and he asked many people to come to a special meal. Important men and officers came. The rulers from Galilee were also there." 
Mark6:22 The daughter of Herodias came in and she danced. Herod and his visitors were very happy when they saw her dance. King Herod said to the girl, ‘Ask me for anything that you want. I will give it to you.’" 
Mark6:23 He made a strong promise, ‘I will give you anything that you ask me to give to you. I will even give you half of everything that I rule over.’" 
Mark6:24 The girl went out and she said to her mother, ‘What should I ask him for?’ Her mother replied, ‘Ask for the head of John the Baptist.’" 
Mark6:24 The girl went out and she said to her mother, ‘What should I ask him for?’ Her mother replied, ‘Ask for the head of John the Baptist.’" 
Mark6:25 The girl went quickly to Herod and she said to him, ‘I want the head of John the Baptist. Put it on a plate! Do it now!’" 
Mark6:25 The girl went quickly to Herod and she said to him, ‘I want the head of John the Baptist. Put it on a plate! Do it now!’" 
Mark6:26 Then Herod felt very sad. Because he had made a special promise, he did not want to refuse her. All his visitors had heard him." 
Mark6:27 So immediately, the king sent a soldier to go to John. The king told the soldier that he must bring John's head. So the soldier went to the prison and he cut off John's head." 
Mark6:27 So immediately, the king sent a soldier to go to John. The king told the soldier that he must bring John's head. So the soldier went to the prison and he cut off John's head." 
Mark6:28 Then he brought it back on a plate. He gave it to the girl, and she gave it to her mother." 
Mark6:29 People told John's disciples about what had happened. So they went to the prison. They took away John's body and they buried it. 
Mark6:29 People told John's disciples about what had happened. So they went to the prison. They took away John's body and they buried it. 
Mark6:30 The apostles all came to Jesus. They told him about all the things that they had done. And they told him what they had taught. 
Mark6:31 Many people were coming to Jesus and then going away. Jesus and his disciples were too busy even to eat. So Jesus said to them, ‘Come with me to a place where there are no other people. We should be alone together. Then we can rest for a short time.’" 
Mark6:32 So they left all the people behind. They went away in a boat together to a quiet place. 
Mark6:33 But many people saw that they were leaving the town. Those people recognized them. So the people ran out from all the towns around there. They went on land and reached the place before Jesus and the disciples. 
Mark6:34 Jesus got out of the boat and he saw a large crowd of people. He felt sorry for them. He thought to himself, ‘These people are like sheep that have no shepherd to take care of them.’ So he began to teach them many things." 
Mark6:35 When it was almost evening time, Jesus' disciples came and said to him, ‘We are in a place where there are no houses. Soon it will be dark." 
Mark6:36 The people do not have anything to eat. So send them away now. Tell them to go to the farms and villages that are near here. Then they can buy some food for themselves to eat.’ 
Mark6:37 Jesus replied, ‘You should give them some food to eat.’ But the disciples said, ‘We cannot go to buy bread for all these people. A man must work for eight months to get the 200 coins that we would need for that. We cannot get enough food to give to these people.’" 
Mark6:38 Jesus asked them, ‘How many loaves of bread do you have? Go and see.’ When they had looked, they told Jesus, ‘We have five loaves and two fish.’" 
Mark6:39 Then Jesus told all the people to sit down on the green grass. He wanted them to sit in large groups. 
Mark6:40 So the people sat down in groups. Each group had 50 or 100 people in it. 
Mark6:41 Then Jesus took the five loaves of bread and the two fish. He looked up to heaven and he thanked God for the food. Then he broke the bread into pieces. He gave the pieces of bread to the disciples, and they gave the bread to the people. Jesus also broke the two fish into pieces for all the people." 
Mark6:42 Everyone ate and they all had enough food. They were not hungry any more. 
Mark6:43 Jesus' disciples then picked up all the bits of food that people had not eaten. They filled 12 baskets with little bits of bread and fish. 
Mark6:44 There were 5,000 men in the crowd that ate the food there." 
Mark6:45 Immediately after this, Jesus said to his disciples, ‘Get into the boat and sail across the lake to Bethsaida.’ Jesus said that he would first send the crowd away. Then he would also leave." 
Mark6:46 So he said ‘goodbye’ to the crowd. Then he went up on a mountain to pray. 
Mark6:47 That evening, the boat with the disciples in it was in the middle of the lake. Jesus was still alone on the land." 
Mark6:48 But he could see his disciples. They were trying to make the boat move along. But it was very difficult for them because the wind was blowing in the other direction. Then, when it was nearly dawn, Jesus walked on the water towards his disciples. He seemed to be going on past them." 
Mark6:49 But they saw that he was walking on the water. They thought, ‘It is a spirit!’ And they screamed loudly." 
Mark6:50 They all saw Jesus and they were very afraid. But immediately, Jesus said to them, ‘Be brave. It is I. Do not be afraid.’" 
Mark6:51 Then Jesus got into the boat to be with them. The strong wind stopped. The disciples were completely surprised about what had happened. 
Mark6:52 They did not understand what Jesus had done with the loaves of bread. They were not ready to learn. 
Mark6:53 They sailed across the lake. They reached the shore at Gennesaret and they tied the boat there. 
Mark6:54 When they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus immediately." 
Mark6:55 They went to tell everyone who lived in places near there. They brought sick people to Jesus. They carried those people to him on mats. Whenever they knew where Jesus was, they brought sick people to him there." 
Mark6:56 Jesus went to villages, towns and farms. Everywhere that he went, they brought sick people to the market places. The sick people asked Jesus for help. They wanted to touch even the edge of his coat. Every sick person who touched him became well." 
Mark7:1 A group of Pharisees and some teachers of God's Law came from Jerusalem to talk with Jesus. 
Mark7:2 They had been watching Jesus' disciples. Some of the disciples did not wash their hands before they ate a meal. That is to say, they did not wash them in the right way." 
Mark7:3 (The Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat food until they have washed their hands carefully. They do this because of what their leaders taught them. 
Mark7:4 When they come from the market place, they always wash themselves carefully. Unless they do this, they do not eat anything. Their leaders also gave them rules about how they should wash cups, pots and metal bowls.)" 
Mark7:5 The Pharisees and the teachers of God's Law said to Jesus, ‘Your disciples do not obey the things that our leaders taught many years ago. Your disciples have not washed their hands in the right way. They are eating their food with unclean hands. That is not right.’" 
Mark7:6 Jesus said to them, ‘You are hypocrites! What God's prophet Isaiah wrote about you long ago is true: God says, “These people say good things about me, but they do not really want to obey me." 
Mark7:7 They say that I am great. But what they say has no purpose. They teach their own rules, which I did not give to them.” ’" 
Mark7:8 Jesus then said, ‘You have stopped obeying God. Instead you obey the ideas that men teach."
