John8:36 So, if the Son makes you free, you will really be free." 
John8:37 I know that you belong to Abraham's family. But you are looking for a way to kill me. That is because you have no place in yourselves for my words. 
John8:38 I speak about the things that my Father has shown me. You do the things that you have heard from your father.’ 
John8:39 They answered, ‘Abraham is our father!’ Jesus replied, ‘If you really belonged to Abraham's family, you would do the same kind of things that Abraham did." 
John8:40 I have told you the true message that I have heard from God. But you want to kill me. Abraham did not do anything like that! 
John8:41 You do the same things that your own father does.’ They said to Jesus, ‘God himself is our only Father, and we are his proper sons!’" 
John8:42 Jesus said to them, ‘If God was your Father, you would love me. I came from God and now I am here with you. I did not come on my own behalf, but God sent me." 
John8:43 Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot really accept my message. 
John8:44 You belong to your father, the devil, and you want to do the things that he wants. From the beginning, he has been someone who murders people. He refuses to accept the truth, because there is nothing true in him. When he tells lies, he is showing what he is really like. He always tells lies. He is the father of all lies." 
John8:45 As for me, I tell you what is true. And that is why you do not believe me." 
John8:46 None of you can show that I have done anything wrong. You should believe me, because I am telling you God's true message." 
John8:47 Someone who belongs to God accepts God's message. But you do not belong to God. That is why you do not accept his message.’ 
John8:48 The Jews answered him, ‘What we say about you is right! You are from Samaria and you have a demon in you!’" 
John8:49 Jesus replied, ‘There is no demon in me. I want people to know how great my Father is. But you want people to think bad things about me." 
John8:50 I myself do not want people to praise me. But there is someone who wants people to praise me. He is the one who decides if I am right. 
John8:51 I tell you this. Anyone who obeys my words will never die.’ 
John8:52 The Jews said, ‘Now we know that a demon lives in you! Abraham and all the prophets died. But you say, “Anyone who obeys my words will never know death!”" 
John8:53 You cannot be greater than our father Abraham, and he died! You cannot be greater than the prophets, and they all died! Who do you think that you are?’" 
John8:54 Jesus replied, ‘If I myself wanted people to praise me, that would be worth nothing. But it is my Father who wants that. And you say that he is your God." 
John8:55 You have never known him, but I know him. I might say, “I do not know him.” But then I would be telling a lie. So I would be like you. But I do know him, and I obey his words." 
John8:56 Your ancestor Abraham knew that I would come at some time. He was happy to know that he would see that time. He did see that time, and he was very happy.’" 
John8:57 The Jews said, ‘You are not 50 years old yet. But you say that you have seen Abraham!’" 
John8:58 Jesus said, ‘I tell you this. Before Abraham was even born, “I am”.’" 
John8:59 Then they picked up stones. They wanted to throw them at him and kill him. But Jesus hid himself from them. He went away from the temple. 
John9:1 While Jesus was walking along, he saw a certain man. This man had been blind since he was born." 
John9:2 Jesus' disciples asked him, ‘Teacher, why was this man blind when he was born? Was it because he himself did something wrong? Or was it because his parents did something wrong?’" 
John9:3 Jesus answered, ‘It was not because either this man or his parents did something wrong. It happened so that God could show his great work in this man." 
John9:4 While it is still day, we must continue to work. God has sent me and we must do his work. We must work now because it will be night soon. Then nobody can work." 
John9:5 While I am still here in the world, I am the world's light.’" 
John9:6 When Jesus had finished speaking, he spat on the ground. He mixed it with dirt on the ground so that he made mud. Then he put some of the mud on the eyes of the blind man." 
John9:7 Jesus said to him, ‘Go and wash in the Siloam pool.’ (The name Siloam means ‘Sent’.) So the man went there and he washed himself. When he returned, now he could see." 
John9:8 People began to talk about the man. Some of these people lived near him. Others had seen him when he was asking for money. They said, ‘This is the man who sat here. He asked people to give him money. Isn't that right?’" 
John9:9 Some people said, ‘Yes, it is him.’ But other people said, ‘No, it is someone who seems to be like him.’ But the man himself said, ‘I am that man.’" 
John9:10 They asked him, ‘How did your eyes now become able to see?’" 
John9:11 He answered, ‘The man called Jesus made some mud. He put the mud on my eyes. Then he sent me to wash in the Siloam pool. So I went there and I washed. Then I could see.’" 
John9:12 They asked him, ‘Where is this man?’ He replied, ‘I do not know.’" 
John9:13 The people brought the man who had been blind to the Pharisees. 
John9:14 It was a Jewish day of rest when Jesus had done this miracle. He had used mud to make the man's eyes able to see. 
John9:15 So the Pharisees asked the man again, ‘How did you become able to see?’ The man replied, ‘Jesus put mud on my eyes. Then I washed, and now I can see.’" 
John9:16 So some of the Pharisees said, ‘This man Jesus cannot have come from God. He does not obey the rules about our day of rest.’ But other Pharisees said, ‘Nobody who is bad could do great things like this!’ So they did not agree with each other." 
John9:17 The Pharisees spoke again to the man who had been blind. They said to him, ‘What do you yourself say about this man? It was your eyes he has now made able to see.’ The man replied, ‘He is a prophet.’" 
John9:18 The Jewish leaders still did not believe that the man had really been blind. They did not want to believe that he had now become able to see. So they told the man's parents to come to them. 
John9:19 They asked the parents, ‘Is this your son? You say, “When he was born, he was blind.” But now he can see. How did this happen?’" 
John9:20 The parents replied, ‘We know that this is our son. And when he was born, he was blind. We know that, too." 
John9:21 But we do not know how he can see now. We do not know who made his eyes able to see. Ask him. He is old enough to answer you for himself!’ 
John9:22 The man's parents said these things because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders. The Jewish leaders did not want anyone to say that Jesus was the Messiah. They would not let anyone like that belong to their meeting places. 
John9:23 That is why the man's parents said, ‘Ask him. He is old enough.’" 
John9:24 So the leaders again spoke to the man who had been blind. They said to him, ‘In front of God, promise that you will speak only true things. We know that this man Jesus does not obey God.’" 
John9:25 The man replied, ‘I do not know whether he obeys God or not. But I do know one thing: I was blind and now I can see.’" 
John9:26 Then they asked him, ‘What did he do to you? How did he make your eyes able to see?’" 
John9:27 The man answered them, ‘I have told you already and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Maybe you want to become his disciples too!’" 
John9:28 Then the leaders were very angry with him. They shouted at him, ‘No, it is you! You are that man's disciple. But we are disciples of Moses." 
John9:29 We know that God spoke to Moses. But who is this man? We do not even know where he comes from.’ 
John9:30 The man answered, ‘That is a very strange thing! You do not know where this man comes from. But he is the one who made my eyes able to see." 
John9:31 We know that God does not listen to people who do not obey him. But he does listen to good people who do what he wants them to do. 
John9:32 Nobody has ever made the eyes of a blind man able to see, if that man had been blind when he was born. Since the world began, that has never happened!" 
John9:33 So this man Jesus must have come from God. Unless he came from God, he could not do anything like that.’" 
John9:34 The Jewish leaders answered him, ‘Since the day that you were born, you have never obeyed God's laws. You cannot teach us anything!’ And they threw him out of the meeting place." 
John9:35 Jesus heard that the Jewish leaders had thrown the man out. So he went and he found the man. Jesus asked him, ‘Do you believe in the Son of Man?’" 
John9:36 The man answered, ‘Sir, please tell me who he is. Then I can believe in him.’" 
John9:37 Jesus said to him, ‘You have already seen him. It is me, the one who is talking to you now.’" 
John9:38 Then the man said, ‘ Lord, now I believe.’ He bent down on his knees and he worshipped Jesus." 
John9:39 Then Jesus said, ‘I came into this world to judge people. Then people who know that they are blind will be able to see. And people who think they can see will become blind.’" 
John9:40 Some of the Pharisees who were there with him heard this. They asked Jesus, ‘Do you mean that we are also blind?’" 
John9:41 Jesus said to them, ‘If you were blind, God would not punish you for the wrong things that you have done. But you say that you can see. You do wrong things with your eyes open, so God will punish you as guilty people.’" 
John10:1 Jesus then said, ‘I tell you this. A shepherd keeps his sheep in a safe place with a wall around it. There is a gate into that safe place. Anyone who gets into that place by another way, not through the gate, is not the shepherd. That person is a robber. He comes to take away the sheep for himself." 
John10:2 But the shepherd goes in through the gate. 
John10:3 The person who watches the gate opens it for the shepherd. The sheep recognize the shepherd's voice. He calls each of his own sheep by their name and he leads them out. 
John10:4 When he has brought out all his own sheep, he goes in front of them. The sheep follow him because they know his voice." 
John10:5 They will not follow a stranger. They will run away from a stranger because they do not recognize his voice.’ 
John10:6 Jesus told this story like a picture to teach the people. But they did not understand what he was saying to them. 
John10:7 So Jesus spoke again. He said, ‘I tell you this. I am like the gate for the sheep." 
John10:8 All other men who came before me were like robbers. But the sheep did not listen to them. 
John10:9 I am like the gate. Everyone who comes in through me will be safe. They will be free to come in and to go out. And they will find plenty of food. 
John10:10 The robber only wants to take away my sheep. He wants to kill them. He comes only to destroy them. But I have come so that they can have true life. And so that they can have everything that they need. 
John10:11 I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd would die so that he can save his sheep. 
John10:12 Another man may take care of sheep so that he gets money. But the sheep do not belong to him. A man like that is not the shepherd. If a wolf comes, a man like that runs away when he sees it. He leaves the sheep in danger. Then the wolf attacks the sheep. It causes them to run away in all directions." 
John10:13 That man runs away because the sheep do not belong to him. He does not think that the sheep are important. 
John10:14 I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep, and they know me." 
John10:15 I know them in the same way that my Father knows me. And they know me in the same way that I know the Father. I will die so that I can save my sheep. 
John10:16 I also have other sheep, and I must bring them too. They do not belong to this group of sheep. But they also will listen to my voice. So all the sheep will become one group, and they will have one shepherd." 
John10:17 The Father loves me because I choose to die for my sheep. But after I give my life like that, I will become alive again." 
John10:18 Nobody can take my life away from me. Instead, I myself can choose to die. I have authority to do that. I also have authority to become alive again. My Father has said that I must do that.’" 
John10:19 Again, the Jews could not agree about these things that Jesus said." 
John10:20 Many of them said, ‘He has a demon in him and he is crazy. You should not listen to him!’" 
John10:21 But other people said, ‘A man with a demon in him could not teach like this! A demon could not make blind people able to see!’" 
John10:22 It was the time for the Jewish Festival called Hanukkah. This happened in Jerusalem. It was winter. 
John10:23 Jesus was walking in the yard of the temple, under a place with a roof. The place was called Solomon's porch." 
John10:24 The Jewish leaders stood around him. They said to him, ‘We want to know who you are. When will you tell us? If you are the Messiah, tell us clearly.’" 
John10:25 Jesus answered, ‘I have already told you, but you do not believe. The things that I do by my Father's authority show you who I am." 
John10:26 But you refuse to believe, because you are not my sheep." 
John10:27 My sheep recognize my voice. I know them and they follow me. 
John10:28 I give them life for ever with God. They will never die. Nobody can ever take them away from me. 
John10:29 My Father has given them to me. He is greater than all things. Nobody can ever take my sheep out of my Father's hand. 
John10:30 My Father and I are one.’ 
John10:31 Then the Jewish leaders picked up stones again to throw at Jesus so that they could kill him. 
John10:32 Jesus said to them, ‘I have done many good things. The Father sent me to do them, and I have shown them to you. Which of those good things make you want to kill me with stones?’" 
John10:33 The Jewish leaders answered, ‘We do not want to kill you because of any good things that you have done. We want to kill you because you are speaking against God. You are only a man, but you are saying that you are God.’" 
John10:34 Jesus answered them, ‘It is written in your own books of God's Law that God said, “You are gods.”" 
John10:35 God called the people to whom he spoke “gods”. And you know that the Bible always remains true. 
John10:36 So, when I said that I am God's Son, why am I wrong? The Father chose me for himself. And he sent me into the world. So you should not say that I am speaking bad things against God." 
John10:37 If I am not doing my Father's work, do not believe me." 
John10:38 But if I am doing his work, you should believe that work. Even if you do not believe me, you should believe the things that I do. Then you will know certainly that the Father is in me. And you will know that I am in the Father.’" 
John10:39 Again, the Jewish leaders tried to take hold of Jesus. But he escaped from them." 
John10:40 After that, Jesus returned across the Jordan River. He went to the place where John had earlier baptized people. Jesus stayed there." 
John10:41 Many people came to him. They said to each other, ‘John did not do any miracles. But everything that he said about this man was true.’" 
John10:42 So, in that place, many people believed in Jesus." 
John11:1 There was a certain man, who was called Lazarus. He lived at Bethany, the village where Mary and her sister Martha lived too. He became ill." 
John11:2 It was this Mary who had poured oil with a nice smell over the feet of the Lord Jesus. Then she had cleaned his feet with her hair. It was her brother Lazarus who was ill. 
John11:3 So the two sisters sent a message to Jesus to say, ‘Lord, the friend that you love is ill.’" 
John11:4 Jesus heard the message. Then he said, ‘This illness will not finish with Lazarus's death. No, its purpose is to show how great God is. Because of this, people will see how great God's Son is.’" 
John11:5 Jesus loved Martha and her sister, and their brother Lazarus, too." 
John11:6 He heard the news that Lazarus was ill. But then he stayed in the place where he was for two more days. 
John11:7 After that, Jesus said to his disciples, ‘We should return to Judea.’" 
John11:7 After that, Jesus said to his disciples, ‘We should return to Judea.’" 
John11:8 The disciples said, ‘Teacher, only a short time ago, the Jews there tried to kill you with stones. You should not return there!’" 
John11:9 Jesus answered, ‘You know that there are 12 hours in the day. Anyone who walks during the day will not fall down. He will not fall down because he sees this world's light." 
John11:10 But anyone who walks during the night will fall down. He will fall down because he has no light.’ 
John11:11 After Jesus said that, he said to them, ‘Our friend Lazarus is sleeping. But I will go there to wake him up.’" 
John11:12 So the disciples said to him, ‘If he is sleeping, Lord, he will get well again.’" 
John11:13 The disciples thought that Jesus was talking about sleep as rest. But Jesus meant that Lazarus had died. 
John11:14 So then, Jesus told them clearly, ‘Lazarus is dead." 
John11:15 But I am happy that I was not there with him. I am happy about that because it will help you. Now you will believe in me. We must go to him now.’ 
John11:16 Thomas, who was called Didymus, said to the other disciples, ‘We should go with our Teacher, so that we can die with him!’" 
John11:17 Jesus arrived at Bethany. He discovered that Lazarus had already been in the grave for four days. 
John11:18 Bethany was about three kilometres from Jerusalem. 
John11:19 Many Jews had come there to visit Martha and Mary. They wanted to help Martha and Mary because their brother had died. 
John11:20 Martha heard the news that Jesus was coming. So she went out to meet him. But Mary stayed in the house with the visitors. 
John11:21 Martha said to Jesus, ‘ Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died." 
John11:22 But even now, I know that God will answer you. He will do whatever you ask him.’" 
John11:23 Jesus told her, ‘Your brother will rise and become alive again.’" 
John11:24 Martha replied, ‘I know that he will rise on the last day, to become alive again.’" 
John11:25 Jesus said to her, ‘I have authority over death. I raise dead people, and I give people true life. Anyone who believes in me will live. Even if that person dies, he will continue to live." 
John11:26 People who believe in me while they are alive will never die. Do you believe that?’ 
John11:27 Martha answered, ‘Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Messiah. You are God's Son, the one that God promised to send into the world.’" 
John11:28 After this, Martha went back to the house. She took Mary to speak to her on her own. Martha said to her, ‘The Teacher is here, and he is asking to meet you.’" 
John11:29 When Mary heard this, she got up immediately. She went out to meet Jesus." 
John11:30 Jesus had not arrived in the village yet. He was still in the place where Martha had met him. 
John11:31 There were still Jews in the house who were with Mary to help her. They saw her get up quickly and go out. So they followed her. They thought that she was going to the place where they had put Lazarus's dead body. They thought that she wanted to go there to weep. 
John11:32 Mary arrived at the place where Jesus was. When she saw Jesus, she bent down low at his feet. She said, ‘Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.’" 
John11:33 Jesus saw that Mary was weeping. He saw that the Jews who had come with her were weeping too. When Jesus saw them, he felt very sad in his spirit. He was upset about Lazarus's death." 
John11:34 He asked them, ‘Where have you put his dead body?’ They answered, ‘Come and see, Lord.’" 
John11:35 Jesus wept. 
John11:36 Then the Jews said to each other, ‘See how much he loved Lazarus!’" 
John11:37 But some of them said, ‘He opened the eyes of the blind man. So surely he would have been able to stop the death of Lazarus.’" 
John11:38 Jesus arrived at the grave where they had put Lazarus's dead body. It was a cave. A large stone covered the front of the hole. Again, Jesus felt very upset." 
John11:39 He said, ‘Take the stone away.’ Martha, the dead man's sister, said to Jesus, ‘But Lord, by now his dead body will have a bad smell. He has been dead for four days!’" 
John11:40 Jesus said to her, ‘I told you already that you must believe in me. Then you will see how great and how powerful God is.’" 
John11:41 So they moved the stone away. Jesus looked up towards the sky. He said, ‘Father, I thank you that you have listened to me." 
John11:42 I know that you listen to me always. But I said this to help all the people who are standing here. I want them to believe that you sent me.’ 
John11:43 When Jesus had said this, he shouted with a loud voice, ‘Lazarus, come out!’" 
John11:44 The dead man came out. There were pieces of cloth around his hands and around his feet. Another piece of cloth was around his face. Jesus said to them, ‘Undo the cloths and let him go.’" 
John11:45 The Jews who had come to visit Mary saw this. They saw what Jesus did. So many of them believed in him. 
John11:46 But some of them went to the Pharisees. They told the Pharisees what Jesus had done. 
John11:47 Then the Pharisees and the leaders of the priests had a meeting with the group of Jewish leaders. They asked each other, ‘What will we do? This man is doing so many miracles." 
John11:48 If we let him do more things like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Roman rulers will come and punish us. Their soldiers will destroy our temple and even destroy our whole country of Israel.’" 
John11:49 One of the group was the leader of the priests that year. He was called Caiaphas. He said, ‘You do not understand the problem." 
John11:50 It is better that one man should die on behalf of our people. That is better than if all Israel's people should die.’ 
John11:51 Caiaphas did not say this because he had thought it by himself. No, he was speaking as a prophet, because he was the leader of the priests that year. That is why he said that Jesus would die on behalf of Israel's people." 
John11:52 And Jesus would die not only for Israel's people. He would also die for all God's people who lived in many different places. He would bring them all together. They would become like one big family. 
John11:53 From that day, the Jewish leaders decided together how they could kill Jesus." 
John11:54 So Jesus stopped travelling about in Judea where everyone could see him. Instead, he went away from there to a town called Ephraim that was near to the wilderness. He stayed there with his disciples." 
John11:54 So Jesus stopped travelling about in Judea where everyone could see him. Instead, he went away from there to a town called Ephraim that was near to the wilderness. He stayed there with his disciples." 
John11:55 It was almost time for the Jewish Passover Festival to begin. Many people were going from their country places to Jerusalem. They were going there to make themselves clean in front of God before they went to the festival. 
John11:56 They were continuing to look for Jesus. While they stood in the yard of the temple, they spoke to each other about him. They asked each other, ‘Do you think that he will come to the festival or not?’" 
John11:57 The leaders of the priests and the Pharisees had spoken to the people about Jesus. They had told them, ‘If anyone knows where Jesus is, you must tell us.’ So then they could take hold of him to put him in a prison." 
John12:1 Six days before the Passover festival, Jesus went to Bethany, the village where Lazarus lived. He was the man who had been dead, but Jesus had raised him." 
John12:2 Some friends prepared a special meal there for Jesus. Martha gave out the food, and Lazarus sat among the visitors, with Jesus." 
John12:3 Then Mary brought a bottle of very expensive oil that had a very nice smell. It was made from pure nard. She poured it over Jesus' feet and then she made his feet dry again with her hair. The sweet smell of the perfume filled the whole house. 
John12:4 Judas Iscariot, one of Jesus' disciples, was there. He was the man who would sell Jesus to Jesus' enemies." 
John12:5 Judas said, ‘We could have sold this oil for 300 coins. That is as much money as someone would get for a year's work. Then we could have given that money to poor people.’" 
John12:6 He did not say this because he really wanted to help the poor people. No, he said it because he wanted the money himself. He kept the bag of money and sometimes he took money from it for himself." 
John12:7 But Jesus said, ‘Do not stop her! She has kept this oil safe until now. She wanted it for the day when they will bury my dead body." 
John12:8 You will always have poor people with you. But you will not always have me with you.’ 
John12:9 By this time, a large crowd of Jews had heard the news that Jesus was at Bethany. So they came there to see him. They came also to see Lazarus, because Jesus had made him alive again." 
John12:10 So the leaders of the priests decided to kill not only Jesus, but Lazarus too." 
John12:11 They wanted to do that because many Jews now refused to obey them. Instead, these Jews believed in Jesus because of what he had done for Lazarus." 
John12:12 The next day, a large crowd of people were in Jerusalem for the festival. They heard the news that Jesus was on the way there." 
John12:13 So they took branches from palm trees and they went out to meet Jesus. They were shouting, ‘We praise God! May the Lord God bless the king who comes with his authority. May he bless the king of Israel!’" 
John12:14 Jesus found a young donkey, and he sat on it. This happened in the way that was written in the Bible long ago:" 
John12:15 ‘Do not be afraid, you people in Zion. Look! Your king is coming. He is riding on a young donkey.’" 
John12:16 Jesus' disciples did not understand all this at that time. They understood only after Jesus had returned to God in heaven. Then they remembered that someone had written these things about him in the Bible. And they remembered that these things had now really happened to him. 
John12:17 The crowd that had been with Jesus before at Bethany continued to tell people about Lazarus. They said, ‘Jesus told Lazarus that he should come out of the grave hole in the rock. He made Lazarus alive again after he had died and his body had been put in there.’" 
John12:18 That is why many people came to meet Jesus. They had heard that he had done this miracle. 
John12:19 So the Pharisees said to each other, ‘There is nothing that we can do to stop this. Look! All the people in the world have left us to go with him!’" 
John12:20 There were some Greek people among the people at the festival. They also had come to worship God. 
John12:21 These Greek people came to Philip, who was from a town in Galilee called Bethsaida. They said to him, ‘Please sir, we want to meet Jesus.’" 
John12:22 Philip went and he told Andrew this. Then Philip and Andrew went to Jesus and they told him. 
John12:23 Jesus said to them, ‘God will show how great the Son of Man is. It is now time for that to happen." 
John12:24 I tell you this. A seed of wheat continues to be only a single seed unless it falls into the ground. It must fall into the ground and then it must die. If it dies like that, it will grow to give a lot of seeds." 
John12:25 Anyone who loves his own life will lose it. But anyone who hates his life in this world will have true life for ever with God. 
John12:26 Anyone who wants to be my servant must follow me. Then he will be with me, where I am. My Father will praise anyone who is my servant." 
John12:27 Now I have trouble in my mind. What will I say? I might say, “Father, save me from this time of great pain!” But this difficult time is the purpose for which I came to the world." 
John12:28 So I will say, “Father, show how great and how good you are.” ’ Then a voice spoke from heaven and said, ‘I have shown how great and how good I am. And I will do it again.’" 
John12:29 The crowd of people who were standing there heard the sound of this voice. They said that it was like the noise of thunder in a storm. But other people said, ‘An angel spoke to him!’" 
John12:30 Jesus said, ‘When this voice spoke, it was not so that it could help me. No, it spoke so that it could help you." 
John12:31 It is time now for God to judge the people in this world. Now he will throw out the ruler of this world. 
John12:32 But when people lift me up from the ground, then I will bring everyone to myself.’" 
John12:33 Jesus said this to show in what way he would soon die. 
John12:34 The crowd said, ‘The Bible tells us that the Messiah will continue for ever. So why do you say, “People must lift up the Son of Man”? Who is this Son of Man?’" 
John12:35 Jesus answered, ‘The light will be with you for only a short time longer. So continue to walk while you still have the light. Do that, so that the dark will not come over you. Anyone who walks in the dark does not know where they are going." 
John12:36 While you still have the light with you, believe in that light. So then you will become children of light.’ When Jesus had said this, he went away. He hid himself from them." 
John12:37 Jesus had done many miracles that the people themselves had seen. But even then, they still did not believe in him." 
John12:38 This showed that the prophet Isaiah had spoken true words when he said: ‘ Lord God, has anyone believed our message? You have shown your great power, but nobody has really understood.’" 
John12:39 Isaiah spoke also about why the people could not believe. 
John12:40 He said: ‘God has caused their eyes to be blind. He has closed their minds. So they cannot see with their eyes, and they cannot understand with their minds. They will not turn to me, so that I can make them well.’" 
John12:41 Long ago, Isaiah saw the great glory of Christ. That is why he said these things. He was speaking about Jesus." 
John12:42 But even at that time, many of the Jewish leaders did believe in Jesus. But they were afraid of the Pharisees. So they did not tell people that they believed. They were afraid that the Pharisees would send them away from their meeting places." 
John12:43 The Pharisees wanted other people to praise them, more than they wanted God to praise them." 
John12:44 Jesus said in a loud voice, ‘Anyone who believes in me does not believe only in me. That person believes also in the one who sent me." 
John12:45 Anyone who looks at me does not see only me. They see also him who sent me. 
John12:46 I have come into the world to be a light for people. Everyone who believes in me will not remain in the dark. That is why I came. 
John12:47 Not everyone who hears my words will obey them. I will not judge anyone who is like that. I did not come to judge the people in the world. I came to save them. 
John12:48 But there is something that will judge people who refuse to believe in me. Those are people who do not accept my message. The message that I have spoken will itself judge people like that on the last day. 
John12:49 The words that I have spoken did not come from me myself. They came from the Father, who sent me. He told me what message I must speak. He told me the words I must say." 
John12:50 The message that the Father has given me causes people to have life for ever. I know that. So I speak only those things that the Father has said to me.’ 
John13:1 It was nearly time for the Passover Festival. Jesus knew that he would soon leave this world and to go to the Father. He had always loved those who were his own people in the world. He continued to love them to the end. 
John13:2 Jesus and his disciples were eating supper. The devil had already put an idea into the mind of Judas Iscariot, Simon's son. The idea was to sell Jesus to his enemies." 
John13:3 Jesus knew that the Father had given him power over everything. He knew that he had come from God. And he knew that he would soon return to God. 
John13:4 So, during the meal, he stood up. He took off the coat that he wore over his other clothes. He tied a piece of cloth around himself." 
John13:5 Then he poured water into a bowl and he began to wash the disciples' feet. Jesus made their feet dry with the cloth that was around him. 
John13:6 Jesus came to Simon Peter. Peter asked him, ‘ Lord, will you really wash my feet?’" 
John13:7 Jesus answered him, ‘You do not understand now what I am doing. But you will understand in the future.’" 
John13:8 Peter said, ‘No! You will never wash my feet!’ Jesus answered, ‘If I do not wash you, you do not belong with me.’" 
John13:9 Simon Peter replied, ‘So Lord, do not wash my feet only! Wash my hands and my head too!’" 
John13:10 Jesus said, ‘A person who has washed his whole body is completely clean. He needs only to wash his feet. And all of you are clean, except one of you.’" 
John13:11 Jesus knew already who would sell him to his enemies. That is why he said, ‘Not every one of you is clean.’" 
John13:12 When he had finished washing the disciples' feet, Jesus put on his coat. He returned to his place at the meal. He asked them, ‘I want you to understand what I have just done for you." 
John13:13 You call me Teacher and Lord, and you are right. That is what I am." 
John13:14 I am your Teacher and your Lord, but I have washed your feet. So you also should wash each other's feet." 
John13:15 I have given you an example. You should do the same for others as I have done for you. 
John13:16 I tell you this. No servant is more important than his master is. No messenger is more important than the person who sent him with a message. 
John13:17 Now you understand these things. So you should do them, and God will bless you.’" 
John13:18 Jesus then said, ‘I am not talking about all of you. I know the people that I have chosen. The Bible says, “Someone who ate food with me has turned against me.”" 
John13:19 I am telling you this now, before it happens. So then, when it does happen, you will believe me. You will believe that “I am who I am”." 
John13:20 I tell you this. Anyone who accepts someone that I send to them is also accepting me. Anyone who accepts me is also accepting my Father God who sent me.’ 
John13:21 After Jesus had said this, he felt very upset. He said to the disciples very seriously, ‘I tell you this. One of you will help my enemies to take hold of me.’" 
John13:22 The disciples looked at each other. They did not know which of them Jesus was speaking about. 
John13:23 The disciple that Jesus loved was sitting very near to Jesus. 
John13:24 Simon Peter looked towards that disciple and said to him, ‘Ask Jesus who he is speaking about.’" 
John13:25 So that disciple moved even nearer to Jesus and he asked, ‘Who is it, Lord?’" 
John13:26 Jesus answered, ‘I will put a piece of bread in the dish of food. Then I will give the bread to him. That is the man.’ So he put a piece of bread in the dish. Then he gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot." 
John13:27 When Judas had taken the bread, Satan came into him. Then Jesus said to Judas, ‘Do quickly what you want to do.’" 
John13:28 None of the other disciples who were sitting at the meal understood this. They did not know why Jesus said this to Judas. 
John13:29 Judas kept the bag of money on behalf of the group of disciples. So some of them thought that Jesus had asked Judas to buy some things. Maybe he should buy what they needed for the festival. Or maybe he should go and give some money to the poor people. 
John13:30 Judas took the bread and he ate it. Then he went out immediately. By now it was night. 
John13:31 When Judas had left, Jesus said, ‘Now people will see how great the Son of Man is. Because of him, they will also see how great God is." 
John13:32 The Son will show how great God is. Then God will take the Son to himself and he will show how great the Son is. He will do that very soon. 
John13:33 My children, I will be with you for only a short time now. You will look for me. But I tell you now what I told the Jews. You cannot go where I am now going." 
John13:34 I give a new rule to you. Love each other. You must love each other as I have loved you. 
John13:35 In this way, everyone will know that you are my disciples. They will know it, if you really love each other.’" 
John13:36 Simon Peter asked him, ‘ Lord, where will you go?’ Jesus replied, ‘You cannot follow now where I will go. But some time after this, you will follow.’" 
John13:37 Peter said, ‘Lord, why can I not go with you now? I would even die for you.’" 
John13:38 Jesus answered, ‘You say that you would die for me. But I tell you this. Soon you will say that you do not know me. Before the cockerel sings early tomorrow morning, you will say that three times.’" 
John14:1 Jesus said to his disciples, ‘Do not be sad or afraid. Believe in God, and also believe in me." 
John14:2 There are many rooms in my Father's house. I will go now so that I can prepare a place for you there. If it was not true, I would not have told you this." 
John14:3 After I have prepared a place for you, I will return. Then I will take you so that you will be with me. You will be where I am." 
John14:4 You know the way to the place where I will go.’ 
John14:5 Thomas said to him, ‘ Lord, we do not know where you will go. So how can we know the way to get there?’" 
John14:6 Jesus answered, ‘I am the way. I am the truth. I am the life. Nobody comes to the Father except by me." 
John14:7 If you really know me, you will also know my Father. From now on, you do know him and you have seen him.’" 
John14:8 Philip said, ‘Lord, show the Father to us. That is all that we need.’" 
John14:9 Jesus answered, ‘I have been with all of you for a long time. But still you do not seem to know me, Philip! Anyone who has seen me has seen the Father. So why do you say, “Show the Father to us”?" 
John14:10 I am in the Father and the Father is in me. You should believe that. The words that I say to you do not come from me myself. But the Father lives in me, and he is doing his work." 
John14:11 I am in the Father and the Father is in me. Believe me when I say that. You have seen the things that I have done. So, if you do not believe my words, believe because of my work." 
John14:12 I tell you this. Whoever believes in me will do the same things that I have been doing. Yes, he will do even greater things than these, because I will go to my Father." 
John14:13 Ask me to help you. If you ask anything in my name, I will do it for you. In that way, the Son will show everyone how great the Father is." 
John14:14 Yes, you should ask me for anything in my name. I will do it for you.’" 
John14:15 Jesus then said, ‘If you love me, do what I have told you to do." 
John14:16 I will ask the Father to help you with this. He will send another Helper to be with you. That Helper will stay with you for ever. 
John14:17 He is God's Spirit and he shows you what is true. The people who belong to this world cannot receive him. That is because they cannot see him. They cannot know who he is. But you know him because he stays with you. And he will be in you. 
John14:18 I will not let you remain alone, like children without parents. I will come to you." 
John14:19 Soon, the people who belong to this world will not see me any more. But you will see me. Because I continue to live, you also will live." 
John14:20 On that day, you will know that I am in my Father. You will know that you are in me and I am in you." 
John14:21 Those people who obey my commands are the ones who really love me. They accept my words and they do what I say. My Father will love everyone who loves me. I also will love them and I will show myself to them.’ 
John14:22 Then Judas (not Judas Iscariot) said, ‘ Lord, why will you show yourself only to us? Why will you not show yourself also to the people who belong to this world?’" 
John14:23 Jesus replied, ‘Everyone who loves me will obey my teaching. My Father will love them. And my Father and I will come to them and we will make our home with them." 
John14:24 Anyone who does not love me will not obey my message. These words that you hear are not my own. No, they come from the Father, who sent me." 
John14:25 I have said these things to you while I am still with you. 
John14:26 But the Father will send the Holy Spirit to be with you. He will be your Helper. He will come in my name, and he will teach you all things. He will help you to remember everything that I have told you." 
John14:27 I will go away but I will give you a gift of peace. It is mine to give and yours to keep. It is not like a gift that this world gives. Do not let yourselves be sad. Do not be afraid. 
John14:28 You heard me say to you, “I will go away, but I will return to you.” If you loved me, you would be happy because of that. You would be happy because I will go to the Father. And he is greater than I am." 
John14:29 I have told you this now, before it happens. So then, when it does happen, you will believe." 
John14:30 I will not talk with you much more, because the ruler of this world will come soon. He has no power over me." 
John14:31 But the people who belong to this world must learn about me. They must know that I love the Father. I do everything that he tells me to do. I must show that to them. Now get up, we must go from here.’" 
John15:1 Jesus said, ‘I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener." 
John15:2 Some branches that are part of me may have no fruit on them. My Father completely removes all those branches. But other branches do have fruit. My Father cuts all those branches to make them better. Then they will be good and make more fruit. 
John15:3 The words that I have spoken to you have made you good already. 
John15:4 Continue to live in me, and I will continue to live in you. A branch cannot make fruit by itself. It can make fruit only if it continues to be part of the vine. You are like that. You cannot make fruit unless you continue to live in me." 
John15:5 I am the vine, and you are the branches. You must continue to live in me and I must continue to live in you. Only if you do that will you make plenty of fruit. That is because you can do nothing without me." 
John15:6 If anyone does not remain in me, that person is like a dead branch. The gardener will throw that branch away and it will die. People throw all those dead branches into the fire so that they burn." 
John15:7 You must remain in me, and my teaching must remain in you. Then you may ask for anything that you want. God will do it for you." 
John15:8 If you make plenty of fruit, that will give honour to my Father. You will show that you are truly my disciples." 
John15:9 I have loved you in the same way that the Father has loved me. Continue to live in a way that shows my love. 
John15:10 If you obey what I have told you, you will be living in my love. In the same way, I have obeyed what my Father has told me to do. In that way, I live in his love." 
John15:11 I want you to be completely happy, in the same way that I myself am happy. That is why I have told you these things." 
John15:12 This is the rule that I give to you: You must love each other, in the same way that I have loved you." 
John15:13 A person really loves his friends if he dies so that they can live. Nobody could love anyone more than that. 
John15:14 And you are my friends, if you obey my message." 
John15:15 A servant does not know what his master is doing. So I do not call you servants any more. No, I call you my friends. This is because I have told you everything that I have heard from my Father." 
John15:16 You did not choose me, but I chose you. I chose you to go and to make plenty of fruit. That kind of fruit will continue always. For that reason, the Father will help you with anything that you ask him. If you ask him in my name, he will do it for you." 
John15:17 This is what I tell you to do: You must love each other.’ 
John15:18 ‘The people who belong to this world may hate you. But remember this: They hated me before they hated you. 
John15:19 If you belonged to this world, this world's people would love you. They would love you because you would belong with them. But I chose you so that you would be separate from this world's people. You do not belong with them, so they hate you." 
John15:20 Remember what I told you: “No servant is more important than his master is.” If they have caused trouble for me, they will also cause trouble for you. But if they have obeyed my message, they will also obey your message." 
John15:21 They will do all these things to you because you belong to me. They will do them because they do not know the one who sent me. 
John15:22 I have come and I have spoken my message to them. If I had not done that, they would not have been guilty of their sin. But now they cannot say, “We have not done anything wrong.”" 
John15:23 Anyone who hates me also hates my Father. 
John15:24 When I was with them, I did many great things. Nobody else ever did things like that. If I had not done those great things among them, they would not have known what is wrong. But they have seen what I did. As a result, they have hated me, and they have hated my Father too." 
John15:25 Their book of God's Law says, “They hated me without any good reason.” What that book already said long ago had to happen." 
John15:26 When I return to my Father, I will send the Helper to you. The Helper is God's Spirit who speaks only true things. He comes to you from the Father. I will send him to you and he will speak true things about me." 
John15:27 You also must speak to people about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.’" 
John16:1 Jesus then said, ‘I have told you all these things, so that you will not turn away from God." 
John16:2 People will send you away from their meeting places. There will be a time when people will want to kill you. When they do this, they will even think that they are doing what God wants." 
John16:3 They will do these things to you because they have never known either the Father or me. 
John16:4 But I have told you this so that you will remember. When they begin to do these things, you will remember that I told you about them already.’ ‘I did not tell you these things at the beginning, because I was with you." 
John16:5 But now I will go to the Father who sent me. But none of you asks me, “Where will you go?”" 
John16:6 Instead, you are now very sad, because I have told you these things." 
John16:7 But what I tell you is true. It will be better for you if I go away. Because, if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you. But if I do go away, I will send him to you." 
John16:8 When he comes, he will show the people of this world what they are like. He will show clearly that they are wrong. They are wrong about what sin really is. They are wrong about who is right with God. They are wrong about how God judges people." 
John16:9 They are wrong about sin, because they do not believe in me." 
John16:10 They are wrong about who is right with God, because I will go to the Father. Then you will not see me again." 
John16:11 They are wrong about how God judges people, because God has already judged Satan, the ruler of this world." 
John16:12 I have many more things to tell you. But you are not strong enough to know them now. 
John16:13 But the Spirit will come. He is the one who shows people true things about God. When he comes, he will be a guide to you. He will help you to understand everything that is true. He will not speak on his own authority. He will tell you only what he hears from the Father. He will tell you about things that will happen after this time." 
John16:14 He will take my message and he will tell it to you. In that way, he will give me honour." 
John16:15 Everything that the Father has is mine. That is why I said, “The Spirit will take what is mine and then he will tell it to you.” ’" 
John16:16 Jesus then said, ‘After a short time, you will not see me any more. But soon after that, you will see me again.’" 
John16:17 Some of his disciples said to each other, ‘What does he mean? He says, “After a short time, you will not see me any more, but soon after that you will see me.” He also says, “It is because I go to the Father.”" 
John16:18 What does he mean when he says, “a short time”? We do not know what he is talking about!’" 
John16:19 Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him about this. So he said to them, ‘I said, “After a short time, you will not see me. But soon after that, you will see me again.” Perhaps you are asking each other about that." 
John16:20 I tell you this. You will be sad and you will cry. But the people who belong to this world will be happy. Yes, you will be sad. But soon after that, you will become happy instead." 
John16:21 It will be like this. When a woman is soon to give birth to a baby, she is sad. She knows that it will be painful. But after the baby is born, she is happy. She forgets her pain, because now she is so happy. She is happy because her child has been born into the world." 
John16:22 It is like that for you. You are sad now. But I will see you again, and then you will be happy. You will be truly happy. Nobody will be able to take away your joy." 
John16:23 When that day arrives, you will not ask me for anything. I tell you this. The Father will help you with anything that you ask him for in my name." 
John16:24 Until now, you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask God, and you will receive what you ask for. Then you will be completely happy." 
John16:25 I have spoken to you with words and stories that are like pictures. But there will be a time when I will not speak like that any more. Instead, I will speak clearly to you about the Father." 
John16:26 When that day arrives, you yourselves will ask the Father in my name. I do not say that I will ask the Father on your behalf." 
John16:27 I will not need to do that, because the Father himself loves you. He loves you because you love me, and you have believed that I came from God." 
John16:28 Yes, I came from the Father, and I came into this world. Now I will leave this world and I will return to the Father.’" 
John16:29 Jesus' disciples said to him, ‘Now you are speaking clearly. You are not speaking with words that are like pictures!" 
John16:30 Now we are sure that you know everything. You do not even need to ask people what they are thinking. Because of this, we believe that you came from God.’" 
John16:31 Jesus answered them, ‘You say that you believe in me now." 
John16:32 But it will happen very soon that all of you will run away to your own homes. Yes, that time has already arrived. You will run away and leave me alone. But I will not really be alone, because the Father is always with me." 
John16:33 I have told you these things so that you will have peace. You will be like that because you live together with me. In this world, you will have trouble. But be brave! I have destroyed the power of this world.’" 
John17:1 After Jesus said this, he looked up to heaven. He said, ‘Father, the time has now arrived. Show everyone how great your Son is. So then I, the Son, can show how great you are." 
John17:2 You gave me authority over all people. You did this so that I could give people life for ever. Those are the people that you gave to me. 
John17:3 What does it mean for people to live with you for ever? It means that they know you, the one who is the only true God. They also know me, Jesus Christ, the one that you have sent." 
John17:4 I have shown people here on the earth how great you are. In that way, I have finished the work that you gave to me to do." 
John17:5 So, Father, now that I return to you, show people how great I am. Before the world began, I had great glory with you. Show again that I am great like that.’" 
John17:6 ‘You gave some people to me who were from this world. I have shown them who you are. They were yours and you gave them to me. They have obeyed your message. 
John17:7 Everything that I have comes from you. Now they know that you gave me everything. 
John17:8 I gave to them the message that you gave to me. They accepted that message. They know certainly that I came from you. They believe that you sent me. 
John17:9 I pray for them. I am not praying for the people who belong to this world. No, I am praying for those people that you have given to me. I am praying for them because they are yours." 
John17:10 Everything that I have is yours. And everything that you have is mine. These people have shown how great I am. 
John17:11 I will not remain in the world any longer. I will return to you now. But they will still be here in the world. Holy Father, please keep them safe in your name. That is the powerful name that you gave to me. Then they can be united in the same way that you and I are united." 
John17:12 While I was with them, I kept them safe in the power of your name. That is the name that you gave to me. I kept them safe so that only one of them turned away from you. That was the man who had to become lost. The Bible already said that it would happen in that way." 
John17:13 Now, Father, I will come to you. I am saying these things while I am still in the world. Then these people, my disciples, can be completely happy. I want them to be completely happy in the same way that I am happy." 
John17:14 I have given your message to them. The world's people have hated them because they do not belong to the world. They do not belong to the world, in the same way that I do not belong to the world." 
John17:15 I am not asking that you will take them out of the world. But I ask you to keep them safe from the devil. 
John17:16 They do not belong to the world, in the same way that I do not belong to the world." 
John17:17 Help them to belong completely to you, as they follow the truth. Your message is the truth." 
John17:18 I have sent them into the world, in the same way that you sent me into the world." 
John17:19 On their behalf I give myself to you, so that I belong completely to you. Then they also can completely belong to you, as they follow the truth.’" 
John17:20 ‘I do not pray only for these people, my disciples. I pray also for those people who will believe in me because of my disciples' message." 
John17:21 I pray that all of them will become united. You, Father, are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be united in us. Then the world's people will believe that you sent me." 
John17:22 I have given to them the same honour that you gave to me. Now they can be united in the same way that you and I are united. 
John17:23 I will be in them, and you will be in me, so that they can be completely united. As a result, the world's people will know that you sent me. They will also know that you love my own people as you love me." 
John17:24 Father, you have given these people to me. I want them to be with me where I am. Then they will see how great I am. You made me great because you loved me. You loved me even before the world began." 
John17:25 Father, you always do what is right. The world's people do not know you, but I know you. These disciples know that you have sent me." 
John17:26 I have shown them what you are like. And I will continue to show them what you are like. Then they will love other people, in the same way that you love me. And I myself will be in them.’" 
John18:1 When Jesus had finished praying, he and his disciples went out. They went across the Kidron Valley. On the other side, there was a garden. Jesus and his disciples went into it." 
John18:2 Judas, who sold Jesus to his enemies, knew the garden. He knew the place because Jesus and his disciples had met there often." 
John18:3 The leaders of the priests and the Pharisees sent a group of soldiers and some officers with Judas. He led these soldiers and officers to the garden. The soldiers had weapons and they carried lights. 
John18:4 Jesus knew everything that would soon happen to him. He went towards them and he asked them, ‘Who are you looking for?’" 
John18:5 They answered, ‘Jesus, who comes from Nazareth.’ Jesus said, ‘That is who I am.’ Judas, who sold Jesus to his enemies, was standing there with the soldiers." 
John18:6 When Jesus said the words, ‘I am,’ the soldiers quickly moved away from him. They fell down to the ground." 
John18:7 So Jesus asked them again, ‘Who are you looking for?’ They said, ‘Jesus, who comes from Nazareth.’" 
John18:8 Jesus replied, ‘I have told you already, that is who I am. Since it is me that you are looking for, let these other men go.’" 
John18:9 Jesus said this because earlier he had said, ‘I have lost none of those people that you gave to me.’ This made what he had spoken earlier really happen." 
John18:10 Simon Peter had a sword. He took hold of it and he attacked the servant of the leader of the priests. He cut off the servant's right ear. The servant's name was Malchus. 
John18:11 Then Jesus said to Peter, ‘Put your sword away in its place! My Father has decided what great pain I must have. I will obey him completely.’" 
John18:12 The group of soldiers, with their captain and the Jewish officers, took hold of Jesus. They tied him with ropes." 
John18:13 They led him first to Annas, who was the father of Caiaphas's wife. Caiaphas was the leader of the priests that year." 
John18:14 It was Caiaphas who had already spoken to the Jewish leaders. He had told them, ‘It is better that one man should die on behalf of the people.’" 
John18:15 Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. That other disciple and the leader of the priests knew each other. So that disciple went with Jesus into the yard of the house of the leader of the priests. 
John18:16 But Peter stayed outside by the gate. Then the other disciple went out again. He spoke to the girl who was watching at the gate. Then he brought Peter into the yard. 
John18:17 The girl who was at the gate asked Peter, ‘Are you another of this man's disciples?’ Peter replied, ‘No, I am not!’" 
John18:18 It was cold. So the servants and the officers had made a fire. They were standing around it to make themselves warm. Peter went to stand with them, so that he could make himself warm too." 
John18:19 The leader of the priests asked Jesus questions about his disciples. He also asked about what Jesus taught. 
John18:20 Jesus answered him, ‘I have always taught in the Jewish meeting places, or at the temple. Those are public places where all the Jews meet together. I have said nothing secretly." 
John18:21 So you do not need to ask me these questions. You can ask the people who heard me when I taught them. They know what I said.’ 
John18:22 When Jesus said this, one of the soldiers hit him on the face. The soldier said, ‘You should not speak to the leader of the priests like that!’" 
John18:23 Jesus replied, ‘If I said something wrong, tell us what it is. But if I said something that is true, you should not have hit me.’" 
John18:24 Then Annas sent Jesus to Caiaphas, the leader of the priests. Jesus still had ropes tied around his arms." 
John18:25 Simon Peter was still standing there by the fire to make himself warm. The other people there said to him, ‘You must also be one of that man's disciples.’ But Peter said, ‘No, I am not.’" 
John18:26 One of the people there was a servant of the leader of the priests. He belonged to the same family as the man whose ear Peter had cut off. This servant said to Peter, ‘I am sure that I saw you in the garden with Jesus.’" 
John18:27 Again, Peter said, ‘No, that is not true.’ Immediately a cockerel sang." 
John18:28 The Jewish leaders took Jesus from Caiaphas's house to the Roman ruler's big house. It was early in the morning. The Jewish leaders wanted to follow the Jewish rules so that they could eat the Passover meal. They did not go into the ruler's house, because it would have made them unclean." 
John18:29 So Pilate, the Roman ruler, went outside to meet them. He asked them, ‘What wrong thing do you say that this man has done?’" 
John18:30 They replied, ‘We would not have brought him to you if he had done nothing wrong.’" 
John18:31 Pilate said to them, ‘You yourselves take him away. You should judge him by your own laws.’ The Jewish leaders replied, ‘We do not have authority to kill anyone when they have done something bad.’" 
John18:32 Jesus had already told people how he would die. These things happened to make his words become true. 
John18:33 Pilate went back inside his palace. He told Jesus to come and stand in front of him. He asked Jesus, ‘Are you the king of the Jews?’" 
John18:34 Jesus said, ‘Is that your own idea, or have other people spoken to you about me?’" 
John18:35 Pilate replied, ‘I am not a Jew, am I? It was your own people and the leaders of your priests who brought you to me. What have you done?’" 
John18:36 Jesus said, ‘My kingdom does not belong to this world. If it did, my disciples would have fought for me. They would have fought so that the Jewish leaders could not take hold of me. No, my kingdom belongs to another place.’" 
John18:37 Pilate said to him, ‘So that means you really are a king!’ Jesus answered, ‘You have said that I am a king! I was born for this reason: I came into the world to tell people the truth about God. Everyone who believes the truth listens to my message.’" 
John18:38 Pilate said, ‘I do not know what truth is.’ When Pilate had said that, he went outside again to the Jewish people there. He said to them, ‘I do not see that this man is guilty of anything wrong. I have no reason to punish him." 
John18:39 But every year we do something for you Jews. We let one man go free from the prison at the time of your Passover festival. Do you want me to let the king of the Jews go free?’ 
John18:40 They shouted their answer, ‘No, we do not want him! Let Barabbas go free!’ (Barabbas was a man who had attacked people and robbed them.)" 
John19:1 Then Pilate told the soldiers to take Jesus outside. He told them to hit Jesus many times with a whip. 
John19:2 The soldiers used some branches with thorns to make a crown for Jesus. They put it on his head. Then they put a purple coat on him too. They did all this as if he was a king. 
John19:3 They came to him many times and they were saying to him, ‘Hello, king of the Jews, you are great!’ At the same time they hit his face with their hands." 
John19:4 Pilate went outside once more. He said to the crowd, ‘Listen to me. I will bring Jesus out here to you. You should know this: I do not say that this man is guilty of anything wrong. I have no reason to punish him.’" 
John19:5 Then Jesus came out. He was still wearing the crown of thorns on his head and the purple coat. Pilate said to them, ‘Look. Here is the man!’" 
John19:6 When the leaders of the priests and their officers saw Jesus, they shouted, ‘Kill him! Kill him on a cross!’ Pilate said to them, ‘Take him for yourselves and kill him on a cross. I can find no reason why I should punish him.’" 
John19:7 The Jews answered, ‘We have a law which says that he must die. He must die because he said, “I am the Son of God.” ’" 
John19:8 When Pilate heard that, he was even more afraid." 
John19:9 He went back into the house. He asked Jesus, ‘Where are you really from?’ But Jesus did not answer him." 
John19:10 Pilate said to him, ‘Are you refusing to speak to me? Remember that I have authority. I can let you go free, or I can let them kill you on a cross.’" 
John19:11 Jesus answered, ‘You could have no power against me unless God had given it to you. So the man who sold me to you has done a worse thing than you have done.’" 
John19:12 From that moment, Pilate tried to let Jesus go free. But the Jewish leaders shouted back, ‘If you let him go, you are not Caesar's friend. Nobody should say that he himself is a king. That shows that he is against Caesar!’" 
John19:13 When Pilate heard those words, he brought Jesus outside. Pilate sat down on a special seat for the judge. The seat was in a place called ‘Gabbatha’ in the Jewish language. There were large flat stones there, which covered the ground." 
John19:14 It was about noon on the day when they prepared the Passover meal. Pilate said to the Jews, ‘Here is your king!’" 
John19:15 But they shouted, ‘Take him away! Take him away! Kill him on a cross!’ Pilate asked them, ‘Do you want me to kill your king on a cross?’ The leaders of the priests answered, ‘Caesar is the only ruler that we call king.’" 
John19:16 Then Pilate gave Jesus to them, so that they could kill him on a cross. The soldiers took hold of Jesus." 
John19:17 He was carrying the cross on which they would kill him. He went out to the place called ‘The Place of the Skull ’. This place is called ‘Golgotha’ in the Jewish language. 
John19:18 When they arrived there, the soldiers fixed Jesus to the cross. They put two other men on crosses near to him. They were on each side of him and Jesus was between them." 
John19:19 Pilate wrote a notice. Then the soldiers put it on the cross. It said: ‘Jesus from Nazareth, the King of the Jews’." 
John19:20 The place where they put Jesus on the cross was near to the city. So many of the Jews read this notice. They could read it because Pilate had written the words in three languages. He had written it in the Jewish language and in the languages of the Roman people and the Greek people. 
John19:21 The leaders of the Jewish priests said to Pilate, ‘Do not write, “The King of the Jews”. Instead, write, “This man said that he is the king of the Jews.” ’" 
John19:22 Pilate answered, ‘I will not change what I have written.’" 
John19:23 After the soldiers had put Jesus on the cross, they took his clothes. They made them into four parts, one part for each soldier. They also took his long shirt, which was one single piece of cloth." 
John19:24 So they said to each other, ‘We will not tear it. Instead, we will play a game with dice to decide who will have the shirt.’ This happened in the way that the Bible had already said: ‘Each of them took some of my clothes. They played a game with dice to win what I was wearing.’ And that is what the soldiers did." 
John19:25 Some women stood near to Jesus while he was on the cross. They were his mother, his mother's sister, Mary the wife of Cleopas, and Mary from Magdala." 
John19:26 Jesus saw his mother there. He also saw the disciple that he loved. That disciple was standing there, near Jesus' mother. So Jesus said to his mother, ‘Woman, look! There is your son.’" 
John19:27 Then he said to the disciple, ‘Look! There is your mother.’ From that time, the disciple took Jesus' mother to live in his own home." 
John19:28 After this, Jesus knew that everything was now finished. Then he said, ‘I am thirsty.’ He said this so that things would happen in the way the Bible already said." 
John19:29 There was a pot full of bitter wine there. So someone put a piece of cloth into the wine. They fixed the cloth to the end of a branch of hyssop. Then they lifted the cloth up to Jesus' mouth. 
John19:30 Jesus drank the wine. Then he said, ‘Everything is finished.’ He bent his head down and he let his spirit go." 
John19:31 It was the day when the Jews prepare everything for their day of rest. The next day was the day for the Passover meal, so it was a very important day. The Jews did not want the men's bodies to stay on the crosses during that day of rest. So they asked Pilate to tell the soldiers to break the legs of those men. Then they could take the dead bodies down from the crosses." 
John19:32 So the soldiers went there. They broke the legs of the two men who were on the crosses next to Jesus. 
John19:33 Then they went to Jesus. They saw that he was dead already. So they did not break his legs. 
John19:34 But one of the soldiers pushed a spear into Jesus' side. Immediately, blood and water came out of his body." 
John19:35 The man who saw these things has spoken about them. What he says is true. He knows that it really happened. He is telling you the truth so that you can believe. 
John19:36 The Bible already said how it would happen. It says: ‘Nobody will break any of his bones.’ 
John19:37 In another place, the Bible says: ‘People will push a spear into that man's body, and then they will look at him.’ And that is how it really happened." 
John19:38 There was a man called Joseph who came from the town of Arimathea. He was one of Jesus' disciples, but he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. So he had not told people that he was a disciple of Jesus. Joseph went to Pilate and asked if he could take Jesus' dead body away. Pilate agreed. So Joseph went there and he took the body away." 
John19:39 Nicodemus went with Joseph. He was the man who had visited Jesus at night. Nicodemus brought about 33 kilograms of spices called myrrh and aloes. 
John19:40 The two men covered Jesus' dead body with these spices. And they tied long pieces of linen cloth around it many times. That is how the Jewish people prepare a dead body before they bury it. 
John19:41 There was a garden near the place where they had killed Jesus on the cross. In that garden there was a place where they could bury the bodies of dead people. It was a new hole in the rock, where nobody had ever put a dead body before." 
John19:42 The next day was the Jewish day of rest. So they put Jesus in that grave hole, because it was near." 
John20:1 Early on the first day of the week, Mary from Magdala went to the grave hole in the rock. It was still dark. She saw that someone had removed the big stone from the front of the hole." 
John20:2 So she ran to where Simon Peter was. He was with the other disciple, the one that Jesus loved. She said to them, ‘Somebody has taken the Lord's body out of the grave. We do not know where they have put him!’" 
John20:3 So Peter and the other disciple started to go to the grave where Jesus' body had been. 
John20:4 Both of them were running. But the other disciple ran faster than Peter ran. So he reached the grave first. 
John20:5 He bent himself down and he looked inside the hole. He saw the pieces of linen cloth that were lying there. But he did not go in. 
John20:6 Simon Peter had run behind the other disciple. When he arrived, he went into the grave hole. He saw the pieces of cloth that were lying there." 
John20:7 Also, he saw the piece of cloth that had been around Jesus' head. This was not in the same place as the long pieces of linen cloth. Someone had put it carefully in a separate place." 
John20:8 The other disciple had reached the grave first, and now he also went inside. He saw and he believed." 
John20:9 They still did not understand what the Bible already said. It said that Jesus had to become alive again after he had died. 
John20:10 Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying. 
John20:11 But Mary stood outside the hole in the rock. She was crying. While she cried, she bent herself down to look inside the grave hole." 
John20:12 She saw two angels there. They were wearing white clothes. They were sitting where Jesus' dead body had been. One angel was sitting where Jesus' head had been. The other angel was sitting where his feet had been. 
John20:13 They asked her, ‘Woman, why are you crying?’ She replied, ‘They have taken away my Lord. I do not know where they have put him.’" 
John20:14 When she had said this, she turned round. She saw a man who was standing there. It was Jesus, but she did not recognize him." 
John20:15 Jesus said to her, ‘Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for?’ Mary thought that he was the gardener. So she said, ‘Sir, if you have carried him away somewhere, please tell me. Tell me where you have put him. Then I will go and I will take him away.’" 
John20:16 Jesus said to her, ‘Mary.’ She turned towards him and she said in the Jewish language, ‘Rabboni!’ (This means ‘Teacher’.)" 
John20:17 Jesus said, ‘Do not hold on to me because I have not gone up to the Father yet. But go to my brothers, the disciples. Tell them, “Now I will go up to my Father. He is also your Father. He is my God, and he is your God.” ’" 
John20:18 Mary from Magdala took this message to the disciples. She went to them and she said, ‘I have seen the Lord!’ Then she told them the things that Jesus had said to her." 
John20:19 On the evening of that same day, the first day of the week, the disciples were meeting together. They had locked the doors of the room because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders. Then Jesus came and he stood among them. He said to them, ‘May peace be with you.’" 
John20:20 After he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples were very, very happy when they saw the Lord." 
John20:21 Jesus said again, ‘May peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, in the same way I am now sending you.’" 
John20:22 When he had said this, he breathed on them. He said, ‘Receive God's Holy Spirit." 
John20:23 If you forgive a person's sins, God will forgive them too. If you do not forgive them, God will not forgive them.’" 
John20:24 One of the 12 disciples was called Thomas. (He was also called ‘the Twin ’.) He was not with the other disciples when Jesus came to them. 
John20:25 So the other disciples told him, ‘We have seen the Lord.’ But Thomas said to them, ‘I will never believe that he is alive again unless I myself see him. I want to see the marks of the nails in his hands. I want to touch where the nails were with my finger. I want to put my hand into his side where the spear went in. If I cannot do that, I will not believe.’" 
John20:26 Eight days after that, the disciples were in the house again. This time Thomas was with them. They had locked the door. But Jesus came and he stood among them. He said to them, ‘May peace be with you.’" 
John20:27 Then he said to Thomas, ‘Put your finger in here. Look at my hands. Put your hand here, in my side. Do not refuse to believe what is true. Now, believe.’" 
John20:28 Thomas answered him, ‘My Lord, and my God!’" 
John20:29 Jesus said to him, ‘You believe because you have seen me. Other people have not seen me, but they do believe in me. God is truly happy with those people.’" 
John20:30 Jesus did many other miracles while the disciples were with him. I have not written about all of them in this book. 
John20:31 But I have written about these things, so that you will be able to believe the truth. You will believe that Jesus is the Messiah, the Son of God. Because you believe in him, you will have life with God." 
John21:1 After that, Jesus showed himself to the disciples again when they were by Lake Tiberias. This is how it happened." 
John21:2 Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin and Nathanael were together. (Nathanael came from Cana in Galilee.) The sons of Zebedee (James and John) and two other disciples were also there." 
John21:2 Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin and Nathanael were together. (Nathanael came from Cana in Galilee.) The sons of Zebedee (James and John) and two other disciples were also there." 
John21:3 Simon Peter said to the other disciples, ‘I will now go and catch fish.’ They replied, ‘We will come with you.’ So they went out and they got into the boat. But they caught nothing during that night." 
John21:4 Early in the morning, Jesus was standing on the shore of the lake. But the disciples did not know that it was Jesus who stood there." 
John21:5 Then Jesus asked them, ‘Friends, have you caught any fish?’ They answered, ‘No!’" 
John21:6 Jesus said, ‘Throw your net out on the right side of the boat. If you do that, you will catch some fish.’ So they threw the net out into the water. Then they caught so many fish that they could not pull the net back into the boat." 
John21:7 The disciple that Jesus loved said to Peter, ‘It is the Lord!’ When Simon Peter heard him say, ‘It is the Lord,’ he put on his coat. (He had been wearing only a few clothes.) Then he jumped into the water." 
John21:8 The other disciples followed him in the boat. They pulled the net that was full of fish behind them. They were not far from the shore. It was about 100 metres away. 
John21:9 When they came to the shore, they saw that someone had lit a fire. Some fish were cooking on it. There was some bread there, too." 
John21:10 Jesus said to them, ‘Bring here some of the fish that you have just caught.’" 
John21:11 Simon Peter got back into the boat and he pulled the net onto the shore. It was full of 153 big fish. There were so many fish, but the net had not broken." 
John21:12 Jesus said to the disciples, ‘Come here and eat breakfast.’ None of them was brave enough to ask him, ‘Who are you?’ They knew that it was the Lord." 
John21:13 So Jesus took the bread and he gave it to them. Then he did the same with the fish. 
John21:14 After Jesus died and became alive again, this was now the third time that he showed himself to his disciples." 
John21:15 When they had finished eating, Jesus spoke to Simon Peter. He said, ‘Simon, son of John, do you love me more than these other disciples love me?’ Peter answered, ‘Yes Lord, you know that I love you.’ Jesus said to him, ‘Feed my lambs.’" 
John21:16 Jesus asked him a second time, ‘Simon, son of John, do you love me?’ Peter answered, ‘Yes Lord, you know that I love you.’ Jesus said to him, ‘Take care of my sheep.’" 
John21:17 Jesus asked him a third time, ‘Simon, son of John, do you love me?’ Peter was sad because Jesus asked him a third time, ‘Do you love me?’ So he said to Jesus, ‘Lord, you know everything. You know that I really love you!’ Jesus said to him, ‘Take care of my sheep." 
John21:18 I tell you this. When you were young, you put on your own clothes. Then you went to any place that you wanted to visit. But when you are old, it will be different. You will hold out your arms and someone else will tie your clothes for you. Then they will take you to a place where you do not want to go.’" 
John21:19 When Jesus said this, he was showing how Peter would die. His death would give honour to God. Then Jesus said to Peter, ‘Follow me.’" 
John21:20 Peter turned round and he saw the disciple that Jesus loved. He was the man who had been very near to Jesus at the supper. He had asked Jesus, ‘Lord, who will sell you to your enemies?’ Now this disciple was following behind them." 
John21:21 When Peter saw him, he asked Jesus, ‘Lord, what will happen to him?’" 
John21:22 Jesus answered him, ‘Perhaps I might want him to live until I return to this earth. But that should not matter to you. You must follow me!’" 
John21:23 At that time, people began to talk about these words that Jesus had said. Some disciples thought that Jesus meant that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say, ‘He will not die.’ Instead, he said to Peter, ‘Perhaps I might want him to live until I return to this earth. But that should not matter to you.’" 
John21:24 I am the disciple who saw these things. I am the one who wrote them down. We all know that what I have written about them is true. 
John21:25 Jesus also did many other things. If people wrote about all those things, there would be very many books. I do not think that the whole world would have enough room for all those books." 
Acts1:1 Jesus did many things when he was living on earth. He also taught people many things. I told you about all these things in the first book that I wrote for you, Theophilus." 
Acts1:2 Those are the things that Jesus did before God took him up into heaven. Jesus had chosen some men to be his apostles. With the power of the Holy Spirit he told them what they must do. 
Acts1:3 After Jesus died on the cross, he became alive again. During the 40 days after that, he showed himself many times to his apostles. He showed himself in different ways. Then they could be really sure that he was alive again. During this time, Jesus spoke to his apostles about the kingdom of God."
